title: Observability intake: runbook & alert-routing sprint plan
preview:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Ada Collins <ada.collins@heliospay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Observability intake: runbook & alert-routing sprint plan

Hi Ada / Helios Ops,

Thanks again for the call yesterday — great progress on the integration plan. To keep momentum, I put together a short intake and proposed sprint plan so our teams can align on dashboards, tracing hooks, error taxonomy, and alert routing. I attached an obs-intake-template.xlsx for your tech leads to fill in.

Quick ask: could you confirm who owns security questionnaire responses and whether we should route alerts to PagerDuty or an internal Ops queue for the first 30 days? Also share any non-standard retention or audit requirements (if you have the doc, please attach).

Proposed kickoff (week of May 25):
- Day 1: Kickoff + environment walkthrough (30–45m)
- Day 2–4: Tracing hooks & dashboard mapping (hands-on pairing)
- Day 5: Runbook draft & alert routing test (cutover plan)

Owners (initial):
- Redwood: Evelyn (project lead), Ben (telemetry), Sean (oncall routing)
- Helios: Ada (primary), Ops Team (tech contacts)

Attachments: obs-intake-template.xlsx (please populate the "service mapping" and "sensitive data" tabs).

If that schedule works I will create a Jira OPS-9821 and a shared Drive folder and assign owners.

Best,
Evelyn Hart
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

---
From: Ada Collins <ada.collins@heliospay.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ops Team <ops@heliospay.com>
Date: 2026-05-17T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability intake: runbook & alert-routing sprint plan

Evelyn — thanks, this looks good. We can start the week of May 25. Ops ownership: I will own the security questionnaire and loop in our compliance lead (cc'd at ops@heliospay.com). For alerts, we want critical incidents to go to PagerDuty and high-volume warnings to a Slack channel initially (we'll add PD escalation after the first week).

Attached our current retention policy (data-retention-policy.docx). We do have an SOC-related addendum that requires 90-day audit logs for request metadata; please confirm Redwood can retain that level in Dedicated config.

Availability for kickoff: Tue May 26 10:00–11:00 PT or Wed May 27 13:00–14:00 PT.

Thanks,
Ada
Director, Infrastructure & Ops
Helios Payments

---
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ada Collins <ada.collins@heliospay.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-18T08:50:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability intake: runbook & alert-routing sprint plan

Looping in internal owners and a quick tech note for Helios:

Ada — thanks for the retention note. Our Dedicated offering supports configurable retention windows; I'll confirm limits for the request-metadata index and whether we need an export policy for the 90-day audit log requirement.

From an implementation perspective I'm proposing the following mapping during the sprint:
- Tracing hooks: instrument the request path at ingress, tokenization, and model invocation layers (we'll provide a code snippet for the SDK).
- Dashboard mapping: map service-level SLI panels (latency P95/P99, error rate by model, tokens/second) and an incidents panel for routing decisions.
- Error taxonomy: classify as user_error, model_failure, infra_error, data_quality. We should add a Helios-specific subtype for "payment_schema_mismatch."

Action items I can pick up before kickoff: provide the SDK tracing sample, pre-populate the dashboard JSON with Helios service names, and open the runbook draft (runbook-draft-v0.1.md) in the Drive folder.

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, CS Ops
Redwood Inference

---
From: Ops Team <ops@heliospay.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-19T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability intake: runbook & alert-routing sprint plan

Thanks — attaching completed security questionnaire and retention policy. We flagged one item where Helios requires HSM-backed key storage for audit log signing; can Redwood confirm HSM integration options? Also, we prefer the first-run alert routing to go to #helios-ops (Slack) and PagerDuty severity=critical.

Attachments: security-questionnaire.pdf, data-retention-policy.docx

—Ops Team
Helios Payments

---
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ada Collins <ada.collins@heliospay.com>, Ops Team <ops@heliospay.com>
Date: 2026-05-20T16:34:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability intake: runbook & alert-routing sprint plan

Summary & next steps (action owners):
- Jira: OPS-9821 created (https://jira.redwood.ai/browse/OPS-9821) — I assigned Ben to prepare the dashboard JSON and SDK tracing snippets by May 22.
- Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcdExampleDriveLink (contains runbook-draft-v0.1.md and obs-intake-template.xlsx). Ben to upload runbook draft.
- Security: Sean to confirm HSM/key-storage options and retention capability for the request-metadata index by EOD May 21.
- Kickoff meeting: Tue May 26 10:00–11:00 PT confirmed — calendar invite to follow.

Open questions for the kickoff:
- Preferred escalation matrix for model_quality vs infra_error? (Helios suggested PD for critical only)
- Any additional compliance artifacts Helios wants attached to runbook (SLA excerpts, dataflow diagrams)?

Thanks everyone — Ada I will include the compliance lead on the invite and attach the completed questionnaire to the Jira ticket.

Best,
Evelyn Hart
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced in thread: obs-intake-template.xlsx, security-questionnaire.pdf, data-retention-policy.docx, runbook-draft-v0.1.md

title: BlueLens: production SLOs, model sizing, and ops plan
preview:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Ariel Soto <ariel@bluelens.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 09:23:00 -0700
Subject: BlueLens discovery — technical deep-dive recap & next steps

Ariel / BlueLens team,

Thanks again for the call this morning — good to meet the platform and ops leads. Below is a short written recap so we have alignment on pain, goals, and concrete next steps. I also attached a short benchmark template (redwood-benchmark-template.xlsx) you can use to share representative payloads.

1) Key pain / goals (what you told us)
- p95 latency target: ~50ms for short, single-turn responses (<=128 tokens) for interactive assistant flows.
- Throughput target: steady-state 400–600 QPS for embedding-heavy microservices; bursts to ~1k QPS during peak batch runs.
- Cost sensitivity: optimize for unit cost (tokens/op) on inference-heavy endpoints; prefer quantized open weights where quality is acceptable.
- Data controls: audit logs and VPC isolation for PII-sensitive inference.

2) Model & workload profile
- Primary model class: instruct-style LLM for retrieval-augmented generation. Proposal: open 70B variant (LlamaX-70B) quantized to int8/4-bit as fallback.
- Typical request: 60–90 tokens input, 60–150 tokens output.
- KV cache reuse: high reuse across short sessions (expect ~70% prefix overlap).

3) Topology & SLO considerations
- Prefer Dedicated pool in us-west with autoscaling and burst capacity to handle peaks.
- Network budget: allow ~10–15ms for network hop to/from Redwood + hardware inference time to meet 50ms p95.
- Batching: recommend dynamic micro-batching for background embedding workloads, and continuous batching disabled on low-latency routes.

4) Proposed next steps
- BlueLens: upload 48–72 hours of representative traffic and 5–10 sample transcripts to the Drive link (I attached the benchmark template).
- Redwood: run preliminary perf runs against a quantized 70B and a 13B variant; share a one-page capacity map and cost estimate.
- Schedule: 60-min ops deep-dive (topology & PoC plan) — propose Thu 5/14 10:00 PT.

If anything above is incorrect or missing, please flag. Otherwise I’ll kick off the perf runs once you share the traffic samples.

Thanks,
Jonas
Jonas Weber | Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: redwood-benchmark-template.xlsx


From: Ariel Soto <ariel@bluelens.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: BlueLens discovery — technical deep-dive recap & next steps

Jonas — thanks for the crisp summary. Confirming the high-level items and adding two clarifications:

- Latency: 50ms p95 is our goal for the chat endpoint; for summarization batch jobs we can accept higher p95 (200–500ms).
- Throughput: embedding microservices are the immediate priority (600 qps sustained). The assistant endpoints will be lower QPS but stricter latency.

We’ll upload traffic samples by EOD Wed and the sample transcripts. Thu 5/14 10:00 PT works for our infra lead (Raj) and me.

One ask — can Redwood include a short note on expected delta in unit cost between 70B int8 vs 13B fp16 so we can model economics for the PoC?

Appreciate it,
Ariel
Ariel Soto
Head of Platform | BlueLens
ariel@bluelens.ai


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Ariel Soto <ariel@bluelens.ai>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 12:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: BlueLens discovery — technical deep-dive recap & next steps

Looping in a couple quick ops notes so we can scope the perf runs correctly:

- Test matrix: please include (a) 70B int8 quantized run, (b) 70B 4-bit fallback, (c) 13B fp16 baseline. Measure p50/p95/p99 for 1–128 token outputs and cost per 1k requests.
- KV cache strategy: measure hit-rate impact with 0%, 50%, and 75% prefix reuse to estimate token/compute savings.
- Burst plan: include an autoscaling sensitivity test — simulate ramp from 200 -> 1k QPS across 30s/5min windows.

I’ll draft the mutual action plan after the perf summary so the customer can sign off on targets and success criteria.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole | Senior Solutions Eng
marissa@redwood.com


From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: Ariel Soto <ariel@bluelens.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 May 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: BlueLens discovery — technical deep-dive recap & next steps

Ariel / Raj — forwarding our internal ops checklist and proposed slots. Sean (cc) will own capacity mapping and cost estimate.

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 09:23:00 -0700
Subject: BlueLens discovery — technical deep-dive recap & next steps
To: Ariel Soto <ariel@bluelens.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>

[original message above]

----------
Jonas
Scheduling note: Thu 5/14 10:00 PT confirmed — sending calendar invite. Sean: can you prepare a short capacity map (nodes, expected GPU types, and cost ranges) for the 70B/13B runs by EOD Wed?

Thanks,
Jonas

title: Request: one-off POC extension + pricing exception for Novapulse
preview:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Bcc: 
Date: 2026-05-12T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: One-off ask: Novapulse POC extension + credits/pricing exception

Team —

Quick one-off escalation for Novapulse (DL-2026-117). Discovery went well; they’ve committed a pilot integration with two product teams and are evaluating for a full purchase expected in Q4. Pricing/legal are close but their procurement timeline means our standard PLG sandbox window (30 days / $5k credits) is insufficient to complete the infra/security review + integration tests.

Ask (one-time, non-repeatable):
- 45 day sandbox extension (starts from current end date 2026-05-28)
- Additional $15,000 in free credits applied to the sandbox account
- Temporary starter discount: 20% on year-1 list if they sign within 120 days (subject to standard commercial review)

Why this is strategic:
- Prospect size: Novapulse forecasts an initial ARR of ~$300k in year 1 if pilot succeeds
- Risk profile: enterprise health-tech vendor with PHI adjacency; longer runway required for compliance sign-off
- Competitive: they’re piloting two vendors; extending runway materially increases our chance to be the default

Attached: draft SOW and completed security questionnaire. Also linking the HubSpot deal (see related links).

Timing: they asked for a decision by Friday 2026-05-14 so they can align legal/procurement.

Recommended routing: Deal Desk + Sean (CFO) sign-off for credits/discount; Jordan to coordinate legal language (SOW addendum). If Sean wants to cap concession, please indicate alternative (e.g., 30 days / $10k) so I can present immediately.

Thanks — Kim

--
Kimberly Park
Account Executive, Mid-Market
Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.ai
Attachments: Novapulse_SOW_draft.pdf, Novapulse_security_qs_completed.xlsx


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-12T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: One-off ask: Novapulse POC extension + credits/pricing exception

Kim —

Thanks for the context, this looks reasonable as a one-off. A few clarifying items before I loop in Legal/CRO:
- Do we have a committed budget / internal sponsor at Novapulse (name/title)?
- Is the $15k pure free credits (no cost to customer) or a credit that converts to invoice if they sign?
- Forecast: what is the probability-weighted ARR and timeline you’re using to justify the concession?

If you can confirm those, I’ll draft a short addendum to the SOW (term extension + credit mechanics) for Legal to review. If Sean wants a smaller concession, say so and I’ll present alternatives.

-Jordan

On 2026-05-12 09:22, Kimberly Park wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick one-off escalation for Novapulse (DL-2026-117). Discovery went well; they’ve committed a pilot integration with two product teams and are evaluating for a full purchase expected in Q4....


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-12T14:37:00-07:00
Subject: Re: One-off ask: Novapulse POC extension + credits/pricing exception

Kim / Jordan —

Appreciate the write-up. I’m amenable to a limited concession to keep this moving, but prefer to set firm guardrails. My provisional approval:
- Approve 30 day extension (to 2026-06-27)
- Approve up to $10,000 in free sandbox credits
- Approve a one-time marketing/customer reference ask in lieu of deeper discounting (so we preserve price integrity)

I’m not comfortable with a 20% starter discount without CRO / CEO sign-off given the size. If the customer can show an approved PO and commitment to an enterprise contract (signature + commercial terms) within the next 60 days, I will escalate to CRO for additional concessions.

Jordan — please prepare an addendum reflecting the 30d / $10k path; if Kimberly reports the prospect will only accept 45d / $15k then we should route to CRO (Rishi / Aditya) for an exception.

Also: include the SOW and security QS in the internal record (done).

- Sean

--
Sean Gallagher
VP, Finance (Acting CFO)
Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.ai


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>

title: Request: Make‑whole & reduced uptime target for Redwood Private pilot

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ari Berman <ari.berman@hearthwell.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Make‑whole & reduced uptime target for Redwood Private pilot

Ari — following up from last week's call. Hearthwell is requesting a 98.0% uptime SLO for the initial 3‑month Redwood Private pilot (single region), with a make‑whole payment structure instead of standard service credits: in short, they want a capped \"makewhole\" (fixed remediation fee per incident tier) rather than open service credits tied to usage.

High level:
- Pilot duration: 3 months (pilot environment in us‑west)
- Expected token volume: See attached hearthwell_predicted_traffic.csv
- Requested uptime SLO: 98.0% (standard is 99.9% for Dedicated/Private)
- Remedy: tiered fixed make‑whole payments (proposed_makewhole_terms.pdf) instead of percentage credits
- Commercial: they are asking for a 22% volume discount on reserved pilot capacity (see redwood_private_pilot_quote.xlsx) if we accept the reduced SLO + make‑whole structure

Marissa — can Deal Desk confirm whether the pricing and make‑whole wording are acceptable operationally? If Deal Desk requires exec signoff for the discount + SLO variation, please escalate. This pilot is strategic for Hearthwell's analytics offering; Ari and procurement want signatures by next Thursday to start the pilot in June.

Thanks —
Kimberly Park
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.com | (415) 555‑0123

Attachments: hearthwell_predicted_traffic.csv (csv), proposed_makewhole_terms.pdf (pdf), redwood_private_pilot_quote.xlsx (xlsx)

Date: Mon, 4 May 2026 15:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: Make‑whole & reduced uptime target for Redwood Private pilot

Kimberly — thanks for the clear packet and attachments. Reviewed with Deal Desk and Legal. Summary of operational/legal concerns:

1) Make‑whole structure: Legal is OK with a fixed remediation schedule for the pilot if we explicitly cap liability and include a one‑way indemnity carveout. We need the final wording in the SOW; see inline comments in proposed_makewhole_terms.pdf.

2) Uptime deviation: Downgrading to 98.0% for a single‑region pilot is operationally acceptable for 3 months provided we add a failover plan and a runbook commitment (we'll require a documented mitigation playbook and a rollback clause).

3) Commercial: 22% discount is above the Deal Desk auto‑approval threshold (15%). Any discount >20% plus an SLO deviation requires an executive approval from the revenue leader or Head of GTM.

Action requested: Lauren (copied below) or another exec needs to sign off on the combined commercial discount and SLO concession. If approved, Deal Desk will prepare the final SOW with the legal language and run the DocuSign route.

Proposed conditional approval steps (Deal Desk):
- Exec approval email with acceptance of proposed_makewhole_terms.pdf and the 22% pilot discount
- Add runbook and rollback clause to SOW
- Limit pilot billing to a capped committed amount (see quote)

Kim — want to flag Sean to ensure we have capacity reservations and a brief runbook review with Platform Ops before we sign.

—Marissa

title: Deployment Annex: redlines, risk carve-outs, and audit trigger
preview:
From: Jill Smith <jill.smith@acmehealth.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.inference>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@acmehealth.com>, Acme Health Legal <legal@acmehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 27 Apr 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Deployment annex — audit trigger and operator access questions

Hi David,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Legal and procurement are moving quickly on the order form but our counsel flagged two items in the draft deployment annex that we need clarified before we can sign:

1) Audit trigger language — the draft gives Acme the right to initiate an audit at 30 days' notice. We need to understand what scope you intend (logs? host access? on-site witness?).
2) Operator access — the redline currently allows operator SSH/API access for troubleshooting; counsel wants stricter guardrails (time-limited jump, KMS audits, and least-privilege).

Can you share a short operational summary of how operator intervention would work for an on-prem deployment, and whether Redwood can accept a limited audit window (e.g., two business days) with scoped artifacts rather than full console access?

We also attached a copy of the current redlines from procurement for reference.

Thanks,
Jill

--
Jill Smith
Director, Strategic Procurement
Acme Health
attachments: Acme_OrderForm_v3_redlines.pdf (pdf)


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.inference>
To: Jill Smith <jill.smith@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 27 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Deployment annex — audit trigger and operator access questions

Jill —

Appreciate the clear ask. Short answers up front, then a proposed operational approach below.

- Audit trigger: Redwood is comfortable with a scoped, time-limited audit window provided we agree on artifacts and a neutral witness process. We are not able to provide open console/root access.
- Operator access: We can support time-bound, logged support sessions via a jump proxy and ephemeral credentials tied to your KMS auditing.

Proposed operational summary (for inclusion in the annex):
- Audit scope: physical access excluded. Audits limited to: system logs (10 days rolling), config manifests for the deployed runtime, and a recorded live troubleshooting session where applicable.
- Notice and window: Acme will provide 7 business days' notice for scheduled audits; for urgent compliance investigations, Redwood will accept a 48-hour notice with a two-business-day execution window.
- Access model: operator troubleshooting performed via temporary jump-host credentials (max 4 hours/session) issued by Redwood's support orchestration and recorded. Any credential issuance is logged and surfaced to Acme's security contact. No persistent root console access will be granted.
- Evidence delivery: where feasible Redwood will provide sanitized snapshots and signed manifests rather than granting live access.

I've attached a short diff (deployment-annex-diff.docx) highlighting the modest wording changes we propose. Marissa/Neha — can you validate we can accept the 48-hour urgent window and provide any commercial tradeoffs?

Thanks,
David

--
David Kim
Head of Sales, Enterprise
Redwood Inference
attachments: deployment-annex-diff.docx (docx)


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.inference>, Jill Smith <jill.smith@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 27 Apr 2026 15:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Deployment annex — audit trigger and operator access questions

David / Jill —

I reviewed the proposed language and added a couple of legal-friendly edits. Key points:

- Narrow the "urgent" audit trigger to defined categories (security incident, subpoena/regulatory inquiry) rather than an open right.
- Replace "recorded live troubleshooting session" with "recorded remote session subject to redaction for customer PII and proprietary artifacts" to avoid forensic leakage.
- Add a clause requiring a mutually agreed SOP for any on-site witnessing requests; Redwood will not accept open-ended on-site audit without a scoped amendment.

I've inline-commented the draft and attached a clean redline for your counsel (see Acme_OrderForm_v3_redlines.pdf). Jill — if your legal team is okay with the narrower urgent trigger and the redaction language, Redwood can accept the two-business-day execution window for urgent audits.

Open item for sales: any customer expectations that require SLA credits or make-good provisions if audit activity impacts availability? If so we need to capture that in the order form.

Neha

--
Neha Kapoor
Counsel, Commercial Contracts
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
To: Jill Smith <jill.smith@acmehealth.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.inference>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.inference>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 28 Apr 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Deployment annex — audit trigger and operator access questions

Quick commercial note from our side:

- We can accept the narrower audit trigger categories Neha suggested. For the 48-hour urgent window, we would request the following customer commitments:
  * Pre-established emergency contact and escalation roster (names + roles).
  * A single-point account-level on-call to validate the urgency.
  * A mutually agreed scope template to be attached to the order form to avoid repeated negotiation.

- Impact on pricing: if an audit requires more than two support-engineer-days of effort for evidence preparation or recorded sessions, we reserve the right to charge professional services time at our standard rates (we will estimate before work begins).

If Acme is comfortable with those commitments I can update the commercial exhibit and reissue the order form for signature. David — please confirm you have internal approval to hold the urgent window as proposed.

Thanks,
Marissa

--
Marissa Cole
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference


From: Jill Smith <jill.smith@acmehealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.inference>
Cc: Acme Health Legal <legal@acmehealth.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Date: Thu, 30 Apr 2026 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Deployment annex — audit trigger and operator access questions

Marissa / David / Neha —

Thanks for the rapid responses and the edited language. Counsel reviewed Neha's edits and is generally agreeable to:

- Narrowing urgent audits to security incidents/regulatory subpoenas;
- Two-business-day execution window for urgent cases with prior validation from Acme's security on-call;
- Recorded sessions with redaction controls and pre-agreed evidence templates.

We will accept the commercial carve-out for >2 engineer-days of work; procurement would like an estimate line on the order form for a not-to-exceed professional services cap for audits (suggest $25k). If Redwood can add that cap as an optional exhibit (customer may choose to opt out), procurement can finalize and route for signature next week.

Next steps:
- Marissa: update the order form to include the optional PS cap exhibit and final annex language (Neha's redline accepted).
- David: confirm internal approval for the two-business-day urgent window and the optional PS cap.
- Neha: I'll ask our counsel to sign the redline exposure and return a countersigned copy once we have the optional exhibit.

Appreciate the teamwork here — looking forward to closing.

Jill

I've attached a short diff (deployment-annex-diff.docx) highlighting the modest wording changes we propose. Marissa/Neha — can you validate we can accept the 48-hour urgent window and provide any commercial tradeoffs?

Thanks,
David

--
David Kim

Redwood Inference

- Narrow the "urgent" audit trigger to defined categories (security incident, subpoena/regulatory inquiry) rather than an open right.
- Replace "recorded live troubleshooting session" with "recorded remote session subject to redaction for customer PII and proprietary artifacts" to avoid forensic leakage.
- Add a clause requiring a mutually agreed SOP for any on-site witnessing requests; Redwood will not accept open-ended on-site audit without a scoped amendment.

I've inline-commented the draft and attached a clean redline for your counsel (see Acme_OrderForm_v3_redlines.pdf). Jill — if your legal team is okay with the narrower urgent trigger and the redaction language, Redwood can accept the two-business-day execution window for urgent audits.

title: CareLink - 429 surge after tier migration
preview:
From: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 07:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 flood since last night

Ava / team,

We're seeing a sharp spike in 429s starting ~01:30 UTC this morning after we moved a large cohort of traffic to the new enterprise tier. Impact: API calls from our ingestion pipeline are being rate-limited, causing downstream processing delays and timeouts for clinical syncs.

Quick details:
- Affected endpoints: /v1/generate and /v1/embeddings
- Sample org/project IDs: org_carelink_7 / proj-ingest-14
- Approx. 20% of requests returned 429, many followed by retries that compounding failures

Can you confirm whether any quota/tiering change rolled out today and whether you can temporarily raise limits while we stabilize? We're prepared to open a maintenance window if needed.

Attaching a small excerpt of request logs for context (full set available on request).

—Tom
Tom Ellis
Head of Platform Engineering | CareLink Health


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>
Cc: CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 08:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 flood since last night

Tom — thanks for the quick heads up. I'm on it and looping in our SRE and Solutions team. Immediate next steps:

1) Can you paste one full request-id and exact timestamp (UTC) for a failing call?
2) We'll check recent tier mapping and quota assignments for org_carelink_7 / proj-ingest-14 and the rollout schedule.
3) If we confirm a misassignment, we'll provision a temporary quota bump and disable the new mapping until we have a safe rollback.

Sean (SE) and Grace (SRE) — can you prioritize an audit of CareLink's org/project tier assignment and the rate-limiter logs from 00:00-04:00 UTC? I may need an exec-level temporary quota increase if this is a customer-impacting misconfiguration.

I'll stand up a short bridge if helpful; otherwise we'll update here within 45 minutes.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 08:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 flood since last night

Adding logs and quick findings. I pulled the audit for the tier migration that hit CareLink at 00:47 UTC: team autoscale pushed org_carelink_7 into the enterprise tier but the project label proj-ingest-14 retained the legacy per-project cap (5 rps). Looks like a label aliasing bug during the rollout (alias proj-ingest -> proj-ingest-14) so the project kept the lower quota.

Evidence attached: throttle_config_export.csv (current per-project caps) and a short sample of the traffic (carelink-traffic-logs-20260320.zip).

Proposed immediate mitigation:
- Temporary increase proj-ingest-14 cap to 50 rps (manual override)
- Rollback the aliasing change and re-run the tier mapping job in dry-run mode
- Throttle back client-side retries using 60s backoff guidance (ask CareLink to patch ingestion pipeline)

Grace — can you confirm whether applying the manual override will trigger any downstream autoscaling churn? I want to ensure we don't create a follow-on capacity surge.

—Sean
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>, CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 09:01:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production errors — 429 surge after tier migration

I reviewed the rate-limiter and routing traces. Confirming Sean's hypothesis: the org-to-tier mapping executed, but our project-label fallback logic matched the old label variant and applied the wrong per-project quota. That created an effective cap far below expected.

Immediate steps I executed:
- Applied manual override: proj-ingest-14 -> 50 rps (effective within 90s).
- Disabled the problematic alias mapping for the live rollout job.
- Monitored for retry storm amplification — currently seeing request success rates climb from 78% -> 96% over 20 minutes.

Caveats / follow-ups:
- We'll need a short scheduled maintenance window to safely re-run the tiering job with corrected label canonicalization.
- Recommend a temporary customer-side retry guidance email: set exponential backoff starting at 15s, cap at 5 attempts. Their current 2s immediate retries are amplifying the issue.

Attaching: throttle_config_export.csv (post-override).

—Grace
SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@carelink.health>, CareLink Ops <ops@carelink.health>
Date: Fri, 20 Mar 2026 13:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production errors — CareLink stabilization and next steps

Aditya — heads up and request for short approval. CareLink experienced a 429 spike due to a tiering label alias bug. Grace applied a manual override (proj-ingest-14 -> 50 rps) and we've seen success rate recover to ~96%.

Asks:
- Approve a one-time customer credit for the incident (recommendation: one week of overage forgiveness capped at $5k) to expedite renewal conversations.
- Approve a limited maintenance window tomorrow 02:00–03:00 UTC to re-run the corrected tiering job and validate label canonicalization in staging.

I'll coordinate customer communications and a short technical postmortem with CareLink once the rollback/retest is complete.

Tom / Ops — appreciate the quick collaboration — we'll send a short bulletin with retry guidance and confirm the maintenance window timing.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (555) 212-0943
Redwood Inference

title: Decision path & kickoff: Stakeholders, procurement steps, and security questionnaire
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@fintera.com>, Tom Briggs <t.briggs@fintera.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Mar 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Decision path & kickoff: Stakeholders, procurement steps, and security questionnaire

Hi Olivia / Tom —

Great speaking with both of you this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment on the buying path and what Redwood needs to start the security scope: 

1) Decision roles (as discussed)
   - Economic buyer: Tom Briggs (CFO) — will sign off on budget & TCO
   - Primary security reviewer: Olivia Chen (Head of InfoSec) — technical questionnaire and evidence vetting
   - Procurement lead: Maya Sutton (Procurement) — terms, PO timing, SOW
   - Legal: internal counsel (Fintera) — contract redlines

2) Systems/contracts in scope for the security review
   - Redwood-hosted inference API (prod and staging)
   - Data at rest & transit controls (S3 buckets, VPC peering)
   - Audit logs & retention (token-level usage logs)
   - Access controls: RBAC, SSO (SAML/OIDC), and service accounts

3) Immediate attachments / asks
   - Attached: redwood_security_questionnaire_v1.xlsx (initial questionnaire) — Olivia, can you confirm the file format is OK or if you prefer a different template?
   - Draft SOW attached for procurement planning: SOW-draft-FINTERA-2026.pdf

4) Proposed timeline / next meeting
   - Intake + questionnaire responses: 1 week (target 3/17)
   - Security kickoff (technical walkthrough w/ Monica Patel, our security lead): 3/18 or 3/19 — please indicate best time
   - Procurement / SOW iteration: target week of 3/22

If that matches your read, please: (a) confirm who's the final signatory for the SOW, (b) add any additional security contacts, and (c) tell us which of the two kickoff times works.

Thanks — looking forward to moving this along.

Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

Attachment: redwood_security_questionnaire_v1.xlsx (xlsx, 42KB)
Attachment: SOW-draft-FINTERA-2026.pdf (pdf, 256KB)

To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Briggs <t.briggs@fintera.com>, Maya Sutton <m.sutton@fintera.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Mar 2026 11:02:00 -0700

Alex —

Thanks for the clear recap. That aligns with our internal mapping. A couple notes: 

- Procurement lead is Maya (copied). She will coordinate POs; she prefers SOW + pricing so she can start budgeting.
- Please add our third-party risk manager, Rahul Desai (rahul.desai@fintera.com), to the questionnaire loop — he’ll want SOC-related evidence. I can share his details if you need.
- For the security kickoff, 3/18 afternoon US Pacific works best. Can Monica Patel lead the technical walkthrough?

We will get the initial questionnaire back by 3/17 with priority on items around encryption, key management (KMS/HSM), and logging/retention. Also want to confirm where logs will be stored and whether Redwood can supply hashed tokens rather than raw PII for audit.

Olivia Chen
Head of Information Security | Fintera

title: Service impact: regional connectivity disruption + temporary routing guidance
preview:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kate Miller <kate.miller@finpay.com>, ops@finpay.com
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Mar 2026 08:23:00 -0700
Subject: Service impact: regional connectivity disruption + temporary routing guidance

Hi Kate / FinPay ops team,

I wanted to make you aware of a regional connectivity disruption we're seeing that is affecting inference traffic for a subset of customers in US, EU and APAC regions. Our telemetry shows requests routed to specific cloud region endpoints intermittently timing out or returning 503s; this is due to a network partition at one of our cloud provider regions that is impacting router control planes.

Immediate mitigation we recommend for latency-sensitive paths: temporarily switch calls to our global fallback endpoints which route around the impacted regions. Example minimal change options (non-breaking):

1) Update host to the global endpoint (recommended for short term):
   curl -X POST https://api-global.redwoodinference.com/v1/generate 
     -H "Authorization: Bearer <KEY>" 
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" 
     -d '{"model":"redwood-std-1","input":"<prompt>"}'

2) Explicit region query param (if you prefer not to change host):
   curl -X POST https://api.redwoodinference.com/v1/generate?region=global 
     -H "Authorization: Bearer <KEY>" -d '{...}'

Notes and caveats:
- No model rollback is required; the fallback routes hit the same model revisions.
- Auth keys and API tokens remain valid; no rotation required.
- You may see a small change in typical latency (100–200ms) during the reroute window.
- Rate limits apply to per-account tiers; if you expect a spike, please tell us and we can temporarily raise your quota.

Attaching a short playbook (reroute_playbook.pdf) and a snippet file you can apply to your infra (reroute_snippets.txt). If you want, we can apply the reroute for you on the account level — please reply with a time window.

We're investigating and will keep this thread updated with ETA and root-cause details. The status page is tracking the incident: https://status.redwoodinference.com/incidents/2026-03-11-regional-network-partition

Apologies for the disruption — we know this affects your production flows and appreciate your patience.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Account Lead, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Kate Miller <kate.miller@finpay.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: ops@finpay.com
Date: Wed, 11 Mar 2026 08:35:12 -07:00
Subject: Re: Service impact: regional connectivity disruption + temporary routing guidance

Thanks Hannah — appreciate the heads up. A couple quick Qs:
- ETA on when the primary region will be functional again?
- Has any customer data been exposed or lost?

We can do the host switch but want to confirm if the changes will affect ordering/timestamps in our request logs (we correlate via request_id). If you can apply the reroute from your side, that would be preferable — can you do it now?

-Kate
Kate Miller
Director, Platform Engineering
FinPay Corp


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kate Miller <kate.miller@finpay.com>, ops@finpay.com
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Mar 2026 08:47:30 -07:00
Subject: Re: Service impact: regional connectivity disruption + temporary routing guidance

Hi Kate — Dylan here from SRE. Short answers first: ETA currently unknown; mitigation in place and we're tracking last-mile BGP flap from provider; we do not see any evidence of data exposure or loss.

Technical details and concrete steps to apply now:
- Cause: transient BGP control-plane instability inside cloud-region eu-central-1 / us-east-1 interconnects causing packet loss to certain availability zones. Our services outside those AZs are healthy.
- Logs: your request_id correlation will remain intact. The host switch keeps request_id and timestamps unchanged — only the outbound path is different.

If you want us to flip routing on your account we can do it immediately. If you prefer to change from your side, here's a minimal on-call snippet for an nginx-based proxy (add to your upstream):

# reroute_snippets.txt (example)
upstream rw_fallback {
  server api-global.redwoodinference.com:443;
}
server {
  location /v1/ {
    proxy_pass https://rw_fallback;
    proxy_set_header Authorization "Bearer $REDWOOD_KEY";
  }
}

We will also temporarily lift a soft throttle for your org while the reroute is active; Hannah, please confirm and we'll apply.

Status page: https://status.redwoodinference.com/incidents/2026-03-11-regional-network-partition

I will follow up with a post-incident summary once we have the root cause fully validated.

Regards,
Dylan Brooks
SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Mar 2026 09:10:05 -07:00
Subject: Re: Service impact: regional connectivity disruption + temporary routing guidance

Internal notes (please keep internal):

Kate asked for ETA and confirmation on data. Dylan confirmed no evidence of data loss. Action items:
- Dylan / Grace: prepare a short postmortem draft (cause, timeline, mitigations) — target EOD today and full RCA within 72 hours. Attach initial notes to the postmortem when ready.
- Sean: authorize one service credit for FinPay for the impacted hour block and flag for finance review (deal id FP-ENTERPRISE-42).
- Vivek: coordinate a 1:1 review with FinPay's security/infra team tomorrow 09:00 PT; offer runbook walk-through.
- Hannah: confirm we will flip account-level routing for FinPay unless they ask otherwise.

I'll reply to Kate once we have the account-level flip in place and add the postmortem link. Please reply on this thread with ETA for the account flip.

Thanks — H.


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kate Miller <kate.miller@finpay.com>, ops@finpay.com
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Mar 2026 17:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Post-incident: summary, reroute confirmation, and next steps (Mar 11)

Hi Kate — following up with a concise post-incident note after today's disruption. Thank you for the patience and for quick coordination.

What happened (brief):
- 08:15–09:05 PT: a network control-plane partition at a cloud provider caused intermittent packet loss to a subset of AZs serving our regional endpoints. That produced elevated 502/503 responses for traffic pinned to those AZs.
- Mitigation: At 08:35 PT we recommended and (with your approval) applied an account-level routing flip to our global fallback endpoint. Your traffic has been routed through healthy regions since ~08:47 PT.

Impact and data integrity:
- Observed: transient request failures and increased latencies for a small subset of requests routed to impacted AZs.
- Not observed: no customer data exposure or persistent data loss. Request-level IDs and timestamps remain intact.

Customer actions taken:
- We performed the account-level flip for FinPay at 08:50 PT as requested. No changes required on your side.

Follow-ups and remediation timeline:
- Postmortem draft: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/internal/incident-postmortems/2026-03-11-regional-network-partition (attachment: incident_postmortem_mar11.pdf)
- RCA completion and technical deep dive: target within 72 hours.
- Credits: Sean will process a one-hour service credit against FP-ENTERPRISE-42 and finance will follow up within 3 business days.
- Optional: we can schedule a runbook/architecture walkthrough; Vivek will propose 1:1 times tomorrow.

Attachments: incident_postmortem_mar11.pdf (summary), reroute_playbook.pdf, reroute_snippets.txt

If you'd like a brief 15–30 minute session to review the timeline and the reroute steps, let us know a time window and we'll set it up.

Again, very sorry for the disruption — we'll get the RCA finished and share root cause and longer-term mitigations as soon as it's ready.

Best regards,
Hannah Schmitt
Account Lead, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

title: RetailCo checkout failures — cascading timeouts after DNS surge
preview:
From: Lisa Tran <lisa.tran@retailco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Feb 2026 08:12:00 -0800
Subject: Checkout failing — 5xx/timeout spike impacting payments

Hi Ben,

We're seeing a sudden spike in checkout failures starting ~07:55 PST. Customers get intermittent 5xx and long timeouts (20s+); our front-end is retrying aggressively which is compounding traffic. Cart abandonment has climbed sharply.

Errors from our logs (sample):
- POST /v1/generate -> 502 Bad Gateway (ALB)
- downstream timeouts to api.redwood.example: context deadline exceeded

title: Data deletion & RTBF — next steps (AcuityHealth)
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acuityhealth.com>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@acuityhealth.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 15:08:00 -0500
Subject: Data deletion & RTBF — next steps (AcuityHealth)

Hi Liam / Priya,

Thanks for taking the time today — great to walk through the integration scenario and the sequence of data flows from submission to indexing. Per our call, you flagged a few important privacy points around deletion / right to be forgotten (RTBF), backups, and proof-of-deletion for audit. I captured the key asks below and included our internal playbook as an attachment for reference.

Summary of asks from AcuityHealth:
- Confirm canonical deletion workflow (API endpoint, console action) and expected timeline until data is unrecoverable.
- Explain backup retention behavior and how/when backups containing customer data are purged.
- Clarify audit evidence we can provide (signed deletion attestations, logs, certificates).
- Data residency: ensure EU region storage for PII and any cross-region replication constraints.

Immediate next steps I propose:
1) Our security lead (cc'd: Sean) will outline the deletion flow and timelines in a reply.
2) Naomi from our Security/Privacy team will provide the audit/logging retention details and any configurable options.
3) If the timeline is acceptable, we can add the deletion SLA language to the DPA and push a light redline.

Attachments: redwood-data-deletion-playbook.pdf (summary + flow diagrams), DPA_Redwood_Inference_v2.pdf (current DPA draft).

Are you available for a 30-minute follow-up early next week to review the details and finalize any DPA language? Proposed slots: Mon 3/1 10:00–10:30 or Tue 3/2 14:00–14:30 CET.

Thanks,

Marissa Cole
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0192


From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acuityhealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@acuityhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 17:12:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Data deletion & RTBF — next steps (AcuityHealth)

Thanks Marissa — useful recap. A couple of clarifications from our side so the legal and security teams can sign off: 

1) API vs Console: We need an automated API-based deletion (so our system can trigger RTBF requests via webhook/API). Is the deletion endpoint synchronous or asynchronous? If async, what status endpoints exist and what guarantees on finalization?

2) Backups: We understand active storage can be purged, but backups are a blocker for us. Our legal req is that once a deletion request is accepted, data must be rendered unrecoverable in backups within 90 days maximum. Can you confirm max backup retention and if there are options to accelerate backup purge on request?

3) Proof of deletion: We need an auditable artifact — e.g., deletion certificate with hash, timestamp, and the retention policy version (DPA clause reference). Do you provide signed attestations or only logs showing delete requests?

4) Cross-region replication: We cannot allow PII to leave the EU. Does Redwood ever replicate backups or snapshots to other regions for DR? If so, can that be disabled per-customer?

5) Logs & Monitoring: What level of detail remains in logs for debugging after deletion? We need at most 180 days of non-PII operational logs; anything containing PII must be scrubbed sooner.

If you can have technical and legal answers to these, we can prepare internal sign-off. We prefer the 3/2 slot.

—Liam
AcuityHealth — Head of Integrations
liam.oconnor@acuityhealth.com


On Thu, 26 Feb 2026 at 17:45 Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.ai> wrote:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acuityhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 17:45:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Data deletion & RTBF — next steps (AcuityHealth)

Hi Liam — thanks for the clear list. Below are concrete, production-accurate answers. I’ve included a short CSV audit template (deletion-audit-template.csv) that we can populate for proof-of-deletion records.

1) Deletion API / flow:
- Endpoint: DELETE https://api.redwood.ai/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/records/{record_id} (authenticated with service key).
- Behavior: The delete call is accepted synchronously (202 Accepted) and the record is immediately marked "deleted" in the primary store. A background job performs physical purging.
- Status: You can poll GET https://api.redwood.ai/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/deletions/{request_id} for status; final state is "purged".
- SLA: Standard background purge completes within 30 calendar days for data in primary region. We can contractually offer an expedited purge (enterprise addendum) to reduce to 7 calendar days for agreed-on scopes.

2) Backups and snapshots:
- Backups are on a customer-tiered retention schedule; default backup retention is 90 days. Backups are immutable snapshots; physical deletion of snapshots occurs at the end of the retention window.
- Acceleration: For EU customers, we offer a "backup purge request" process that enqueues a snapshot rewrite to exclude deleted objects; this typically completes within 60–90 days depending on snapshot cadence and replication.
- Cross-region DR: By default, for EU-region tenants we do not replicate PII-containing snapshots outside the EU unless customer opts into global DR. We can make replication opt-in in the tenant config.

3) Proof of deletion / audit evidence:
- We can provide a signed deletion certificate containing: tenant_id, record_id(s), request_id, deletion timestamp, purged timestamp(s), and SHA256 of object identifiers. Certificates are signed with our HSM-backed key and provided as PDF.
- Additionally, we can provide the populated deletion-audit-template.csv and redacted system logs showing the lifecycle events.

4) Logs & Retention:
- Operational logs (non-PII) retained for 180 days by default and are available via our Console.
- Access logs that could contain PII are retained for 30 days unless a longer window is contracted; we support scrubbing strategies and field-level redaction.

5) Practical constraints:
- Immutable third-party backups (customer-managed backups exported by their tooling) are outside Redwood's control; if Acuity exports snapshots, those need coordination.
- Recovery windows: once physical purge completes we cannot recover the objects. The purge job handles associated KV cache and derived embeddings cleanup.

Attachments included: deletion-audit-template.csv (empty template). I also attached the internal playbook overview to show diagrams of the flow — marissa already shared that.

If you want, we can draft the DPA clause text specifying the 30/90 default with an optional expedited 7-day purge for contracted enterprise customers.

—Naomi Feldman
Director, Security & Compliance | Redwood Inference
naomi_feldman@redwood.ai


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@acuityhealth.com>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@acuityhealth.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 27 Feb 2026 10:23:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Data deletion & RTBF — next steps (AcuityHealth)

Liam — great, thanks Naomi for the detailed notes.

Recap and proposed next steps: 
- Default: primary-store purge within 30 days; backups removed within up to 90 days.
- Optional: expedited enterprise purge to 7 days (requires addendum).
- Proof: signed deletion certificate + populated CSV audit.
- Data residency: EU-only storage; cross-region replication disabled by default for PII.

Please confirm if the 30/90 default meets AcuityHealth's legal bar or whether you need the expedited 7-day option. If you need the faster timeline, I’ll loop in our legal and propose contract language and pricing for the addendum.

Also, I’ve tentatively booked a 30-minute follow-up on Tue 3/2 14:00 CET — please accept if that works. I’ll bring Naomi and Sean to walk the technical bits and DPA language.

Thanks,
Marissa
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0192

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodai.com>
To: Alexa Ruiz <alexa@fintechops.com>

Date: Tue, 24 Feb 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: FinTech Ops pilot: billing qualification, unit-econ targets & credit extension request

Hi Alexa — great to meet you on the call earlier. Quick recap and next steps on the topics we covered (billing and pilot credits):

1) Estimated request profile (your numbers):
- 1M requests/day peak for event enrichment (typical prompt ~75 tokens input + 50 tokens output)
- Batch offline scoring: ~500k calls/day at ~30 tokens each
- Rough monthly aggregate: ~1.8–2.4B tokens/month (range depends on sampling and dedup)

2) Unit economics target:
- You mentioned a target of ~$18–22 per 1M tokens for production (including baseline caching and quantized model).
- For the pilot we proposed a target ceiling of $35/1M tokens while we tune cache/batch settings.

3) Pilot credits:
- To cover initial ramp and allow us to tune the caching/batching, would you consider a 2–3 week trial with a Redwood trial credit top-up? Please confirm the target coverage (tokens or $) you need.

Attachments: I’m attaching a usage projection (usage_projection_finops.xlsx) and a draft pilot plan (pilot_plan_v2.pdf) with a proposed cadence for checkpoints.

Can you confirm the monthly token range we should size for the pilot (low / nominal / high) and whether procurement needs us to route any paperwork to procurement@fintechops.com? Aditya Rao

Procurement note: please send any pilot credit agreement or PO request to procurement@fintechops.com. They’ll need the credit amount expressed as a dollar cap and a short SOW.

For now we’d like to request a trial credit that covers approximately 2.5M tokens (we budget token requests in 1M increments) — effectively a ~2.5M token credit to give us headroom for initial testing and tuning. title: URGENT: Production latency spike — RCA & closure plan for VoltRetail
preview:
From: Michael Chen <michael@voltretail.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karla Ruiz <karla@voltretail.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Latency spike — checkout API 09:05 PST (prod)

Dylan,

We observed a significant latency spike and elevated 5xx rates on our checkout flow this morning around 09:05 PST. Page load times jumped to ~1.8s for the generation call that usually completes in ~200ms, and customers saw errors intermittently. This impacted two of our largest stores and resulted in ~7% checkout abandonment for a 10-minute window.

Attached is our capture summary and a sample request id: req_20260218_0905_41. We need an incident summary and ETA for root cause and remediation — our execs are asking for formal RCA and any financial relief.

Thanks,
Michael Chen
Head of Engineering
VoltRetail Inc.

Attachment: volt_incident_capture.json (summary)


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Hiro Tanaka <hiro.tanaka@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 09:30:12 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Latency spike — checkout API 09:05 PST (prod)

Team — forwarding VoltRetail's report. Key asks: RCA, corrective actions, and a closure plan for their execs. High priority.

Initial observations: our monitoring shows a short-lived queueing spike on the inference pool for the region that aligns to their timeframe. Need SRE to validate whether routing change or cache pressure caused the tail latency. Evelyn, please coordinate customer comms; Rachel/Sean — heads-up on potential credit ask.

Dylan Brooks
Sr. Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachment: volt_incident_routing_log.csv (attached by customer)


From: Hiro Tanaka <hiro.tanaka@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 11:04:45 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Latency spike — checkout API 09:05 PST (prod)

On Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 09:30 AM Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team — forwarding VoltRetail's report. Key asks: RCA, corrective actions, and a closure plan for their execs. High priority.

Checked logs and instance metrics. Preliminary findings:
- Between 09:03–09:08 PST we saw a transient surge of requests pinned to a small subset of serving workers (shard 7).
- Root contributor appears to be an overly-aggressive routing fallback: when a new model variant was rolled into the pool at 09:00, a subset of requests were re-routed repeatedly during warmup causing KV cache churn and longer kernel selection paths.
- CPU/GPU utilization peaked but did not show OOMs; tail latency due to cache misses and repeated model load attempts.

Immediate mitigations applied at 09:15:
1) Rebalanced request routing to avoid shard 7 for high-throughput routes.
2) Warmup window for the model variant was extended and a guard added to reject rapid re-routes.
3) Temporarily pinned VoltRetail's route to the previous stable variant for 2 hours.

I'll prepare a short RCA draft and run a deeper trace to confirm the chain of events. Will attach the trace bundle in the next update.

- Hiro
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Michael Chen <michael@voltretail.com>, Karla Ruiz <karla@voltretail.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Hiro Tanaka <hiro.tanaka@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 19 Feb 2026 08:22:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Latency spike — checkout API 09:05 PST (prod)

Michael / Karla — thank you for the report and sample IDs. Quick update: our SREs attribute the spike to a routing/warmup regression introduced with a model-variant rollout. We applied mitigations and pinned your route to the stable variant.

Attached is a draft RCA for your review (high level) and a proposed corrective plan:
- Finalize RCA (this week) — root cause: routing fallback + warmup policy gap causing KV cache churn.
- Near-term: stricter rollout gating and per-route warmup budgets (done for your route).
- Mid-term (2–4 weeks): add rollback-safe routing logic and extend kernel-selection telemetry.
- Customer ask: propose a courtesy credit for the impacted window; we'll propose numbers after internal review.

Requesting your execs' preferred format for the RCA (PDF + summary email / slide). We can schedule a 30-min readout at your convenience.

Best,
Dylan Brooks
Sr. Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachment: rca-draft.pdf


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Feb 2026 09:10:05 -0800
Subject: Re: RE: Latency spike — checkout API 09:05 PST (prod)

Thanks for the fast recovery work. A few items from exec:
- Approve up to $5k in account credit (pending final impact calc) — Rachel, please own the credit proposal and legal coordination if needed.
- We should provide VoltRetail a clear follow-up schedule with owners and dates; include Product + SRE deliverables.
- I want a one-page executive summary by EOD Tue that they can share internally.

Dylan/Evelyn — please confirm owners and dates in a single tracker item (use the mitigation-checklist.xlsx). I want weekly short check-ins until closure.

Sean Gallagher
VP Sales, Redwood Inference


From: Michael Chen <michael@voltretail.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karla Ruiz <karla@voltretail.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 23 Feb 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: RE: Latency spike — checkout API 09:05 PST (prod)

Appreciate the clear answers and the proposed plan. PDF summary is perfect for our execs. Two quick asks:
1) Can you include the exact customer-impact window and method for how you calculated abandonment impact? Our finance team will need that for the credit conversation.
2) Schedule the readout for Tue 24 Feb 09:00 PST if possible — Karla will join.

Once we have the one-pager and the mitigation tracker populated, we can close this out on our side.

Thanks,
Michael Chen
Head of Engineering
VoltRetail Inc.

title: Release train notes (Feb 24): Auto-rollback on SLO breach — feature flag defaults + staged enablement + backout
preview:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 19, 2026 at 4:12 PM PST
Subject: Release train notes (Feb 24): Auto-rollback on SLO breach — feature flag defaults + staged enablement + backout

Hi Marissa / Sean,

Putting together release train notes for next week (Feb 24 train) and want to confirm the final posture for the auto-rollback on SLO breach integration.

Please confirm / correct the below so we can publish it to the train channel + the release doc:

1) Feature flags + defaults
- rollout.slo_eval.enabled
  - dev/staging: ON
  - prod: ON (evaluation only) OR OFF? (need decision)
- rollout.auto_action.enabled (executor: pause/rollback)
  - dev/staging: ON
  - prod: OFF by default
- rollout.auto_action.mode
  - staging: rollback_allowed
  - prod: pause_only for first week? (or rollback_allowed but gated by allowlist)

2) Staged enablement proposal
- Staging: enable for internal canary route(s) immediately after deployment
- Prod: enable evaluation (no action) for 24h, then enable pause_only for 1 internal route, then allowlist expansion
- Region order: us-west-2 -> us-east-1 -> eu-west-1

3) Backout
- Single switch: rollout.auto_action.enabled=OFF globally
- If evaluator is causing controller load, also flip rollout.slo_eval.enabled=OFF
- Roll back controller build only if flags don’t stabilize

If you can reply with the final answers (esp. prod defaults + first prod canary), I’ll lock the train notes and attach the checklist.

Thanks,

title: Integration follow-up: schema & runtime semantics for SDK/streaming
preview:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rao <aisha@acme.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Feb 2026 15:04:00 -0800
Subject: Integration follow-up: schema & runtime semantics for SDK/streaming

Hi Aisha —

Great call earlier — appreciate you and the Dev Platform team walking through your integration goals. Quick recap and a short list of concrete questions so we can give a focused tech proposal and sample config: 

Recap (what we heard)
- You want the Redwood SDK embedded in Acme's service mesh to route inference requests to our Hosted and (later) Dedicated endpoints depending on data residency and cost rules.
- Primary use cases: multi-turn customer support with tool calling, on-demand semantic search + reranking for product docs, and batched embeddings for analytics.
- Security: must support short-lived credentials and audit logging; on-prem option for PII flows.

Immediate asks / clarifications we promised to confirm
1) Chat vs completions: for multi-turn tool calling and structured output, is your team OK with a chat-style request schema (messages array) or do you prefer single-request completions? Any constraints from your existing chat-state store?
2) Tokenization and limits: what tokenizers are you standardizing on for counting units (we can provide tokenization snapshots)? What's your target max_tokens for generated output in the support flow (per-turn and session-level)?
3) Streaming semantics: do you need character-by-character delta streaming (SSE/chunked) or is single-response ok? How should we encode stop sequences when streaming?
4) Embeddings/reranker shape: expected embedding dimension, and whether you plan to precompute or stream embeddings at query-time for reranking.
5) Auth: do you prefer long-lived API keys on your side, or a short-lived token/oauth flow with a gateway that mints tokens? Any preference around mutual TLS?

Attachments: attached the meeting MPA (acme-integration-mpa.pdf) and a lightweight starter config.

Next steps: if you can reply with answers inline, we will: (a) produce an SDK config example (node/python) that shows chat vs completions payloads, streaming example, and an auth flow sketch; (b) propose routing rules for Hosted vs Dedicated; (c) draft a simple eval set for reranker accuracy checks.

Thanks — I’ll coordinate the sample and send a short follow-up call invite after your responses.

Connor
Account Exec — Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0132

--
Attachment: acme-integration-mpa.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Aisha Rao <aisha@acme.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Dev Platform Team <dev-platform@acme.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Integration follow-up: schema & runtime semantics for SDK/streaming

Connor —

Thanks for the thorough recap. Quick answers and a few clarifying questions from our engineering team: 

1) Chat vs completions
- We prefer a chat-style request for the multi-turn support flow so we can attach metadata to each message (agent id, channel, conversation state). For single-turn template generation we can use completions.

2) Tokenization
- We're standardizing on the OpenAI-compatible tokenizer count (for billing/quotas). Can you confirm Redwood's token counting aligns with that? Is there an API to pre-tokenize or estimate tokens client-side?

3) max_tokens / stop
- For support agent responses we currently cap generated response at ~512 tokens per turn. We need explicit stop sequence support (e.g., "\
--END-RESPONSE--") because our agent framework expects a deterministic terminator.

4) Streaming
- We want streaming to show partial deltas in the UI. Prefer event-delimited streaming (SSE or HTTP/2 chunking). If a stop sequence is emitted mid-stream, how does Redwood guarantee the stream terminates cleanly and how do we detect truncated outputs (length caps)?

5) Embeddings/reranker
- Embedding dim target is 1024. We'll do approximate ANN for retrieval; reranking is per-top-50. Can Redwood run batched reranking on your side (send scores back) or should we compute re-ranks locally?

6) Auth
- We prefer a short-lived token minted by our gateway. mTLS would be negotiable for Dedicated/Private. Also, do your SDKs support token refresh hooks?

Attached: acme-sample-app-design.zip (contains architecture sketch).

Can you provide a minimal sample request/response (chat + streaming) and a note on how stop tokens work with streaming and SSE events? That will help our frontend and infra teams.

Best,
Aisha Rao
Head of Dev Platform — Acme Corp
aisha@acme.com

--
Attachment: acme-sample-app-design.zip (application/zip)


From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rao <aisha@acme.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 11:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Integration follow-up: schema & runtime semantics for SDK/streaming

Hi Aisha —

Thanks — below are specific answers and example payloads to make this actionable. I’ll also attach a short SDK snippet and push a repo sample to the Drive link in the next 24 hours.

High-level guidance
- Use chat API for multi-turn + tool calling/structured output. Completions fine for stateless, single-shot templates. Chat schema (messages[] with role/name) enables our tool-calling and structured JSON output checks.
- Token counting: Redwood exposes an endpoint /tokens/estimate and SDK helpers (tokenizer.count(text)) that use the same tokenizer as our billing so counts align with OpenAI-compatible tokenizers. We’ll add a sample util to the repo.
- max_tokens: this is a generation cap (applies to output tokens only). Input tokens count against context window but are not truncated by max_tokens. If total input+requested output exceeds model limit, the request will be rejected with a 413-like error; SDKs can auto-shorten or fail-fast depending on config.

Streaming semantics (practical notes)
- We support SSE and HTTP/2 chunked streaming depending on SDK and environment. Streaming sends incremental \"delta\" events with partial text, and a final \"done\" event.
- Stop sequences: server will emit the final delta that contains the stop sequence and then send the final \"done\" event and close. On SSE the final event contains {"type":"done","reason":"stop_sequence"}. If a hard max_tokens truncation happens we send {"type":"done","reason":"length"} so you can detect truncation.
- Client-side: watch for the done reason and the last chunk to determine if output is complete or truncated. SDKs expose a \"finishedReason\" on the final response object.

Embeddings + reranking
- Model dims: we have embeddings models at 768 and 1024. Batch embeddings is supported (send up to 256 inputs/req).
- Reranking: we can run a batched cross-encoder reranker on Redwood side and return scores (recommended for consistency and to reduce latency on large result sets). We'll show a sample API call: POST /v1/rerank with candidates + query -> returns scores and optional re-ordered IDs.

Auth & deployment
- Recommended: short-lived bearer tokens minted by your gateway (JWT with 5–15 min TTL) + SDK refresh hook. For Dedicated/Private you can add mTLS. SDKs include a tokenProvider callback to fetch/refresh credentials.

Minimal examples (pseudo payloads)
- Chat request (single-turn, JSON):
  {
    \"model\":\"redwood-chat-1\",
    \"messages\":[{\"role\":\"system\",\"content\":\"You are a helpful support assistant.\"},{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"Issue: login fails with 403\"}],
    \"stream\": false,
    \"max_tokens\": 512,
    \"stop\":[\"\
--END-RESPONSE--\"]
  }

- Streaming (SSE) behavior (client receives events):
  event: delta
  data: {\"role\":\"assistant\",\"delta\":\"We recommend resetting your token...\"}

  event: delta
  data: {\"role\":\"assistant\",\"delta\":\"...then check the auth header.\"}

  event: done
  data: {\"finishedReason\":\"stop_sequence\"}

- Embeddings batch (JSON):
  POST /v1/embeddings
  {\"model\":\"redwood-embed-1024\",\"input\":[\"doc1 text\",\"doc2 text\"]}

Edge cases
- If you receive a done/reason:length, you can re-issue a chained request with last-n tokens as context for continuation. Our SDK has helpers for continuation tokens.
- When routing between Hosted and Dedicated, prefix cache keys with \"dedicated:<team>\" to avoid cache pollution.

Next actions from us
1) I will send a small SDK example (node + python) showing chat + streaming + tokenProvider.
2) Marissa will draft a sample reranker flow and estimated cost for hosted reranking vs local re-rank.
3) We’ll propose a follow-up call next week to walk your infra team through token minting.

Connor
Account Exec — Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.com

--


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rao <aisha@acme.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Feb 2026 08:01:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Integration follow-up: schema & runtime semantics for SDK/streaming

Aisha / team —

Thanks for the clear requirements. Internal follow-up and owners: 
- Marissa: put together a sample Node SDK config + HubSpot docs and estimate (due: 2026-02-20).
- Sean: prototype a reranker-hosted vs local costing doc and rough latency numbers (due: 2026-02-22).
- Connor: schedule the technical token-minting call (will propose 30-min slot).

A couple of quick clarifications we need before the SDK sample:
- Confirm you want SSE for streaming (we'll prioritize SSE sample) vs websocket.
- Confirm whether the stop token \"\
--END-RESPONSE--\" is sufficient or if you need multiple variants.

I'll attach the initial Node sample and HubSpot link once ready. Would you like us to open a DocuSign for a pilot reserved capacity quote or prefer to wait till after the architecture review?

Cheers,
Marissa Cole
Solutions Engineering — Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

--

From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rao <aisha@acme.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Feb 2026 11:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Integration follow-up: schema & runtime semantics for SDK/streaming

Hi Aisha —

Thanks — below are specific answers and example payloads to make this actionable. I’ll also attach a short SDK snippet and push a repo sample to the Drive link in the next 24 hours.

High-level guidance
- Use chat API for multi-turn + tool calling/structured output. Completions fine for stateless, single-shot templates. Chat schema (messages[] with role/name) enables our tool-calling and structured JSON output checks.
- Token counting: Redwood exposes an endpoint /tokens/estimate and SDK helpers (tokenizer.count(text)) that use the same tokenizer as our billing so counts align with OpenAI-compatible tokenizers. We’ll add a sample util to the repo.
- max_tokens: this is a generation cap (applies to output tokens only). Input tokens count against context window but are not truncated by max_tokens. If total input+requested output exceeds model limit, the request will be rejected with a 413-like error; SDKs can auto-shorten or fail-fast depending on config.

Streaming semantics (practical notes)
- We support SSE and HTTP/2 chunked streaming depending on SDK and environment. Streaming sends incremental \"delta\" events with partial text, and a final \"done\" event.
- Stop sequences: server will emit the final delta that contains the stop sequence and then send the final \"done\" event and close. On SSE the final event contains {"type":"done","reason":"stop_sequence"}. If a hard max_tokens truncation happens we send {"type":"done","reason":"length"} so you can detect truncation.
- Client-side: watch for the done reason and the last chunk to determine if output is complete or truncated. SDKs expose a \"finishedReason\" on the final response object.

Embeddings + reranking
- Model dims: we have embeddings models at 768 and 1024. Batch embeddings is supported (send up to 256 inputs/req).
- Reranking: we can run a batched cross-encoder reranker on Redwood side and return scores (recommended for consistency and to reduce latency on large result sets). We'll show a sample API call: POST /v1/rerank with candidates + query -> returns scores and optional re-ordered IDs.

Auth & deployment
- Recommended: short-lived bearer tokens minted by your gateway (JWT with 5–15 min TTL) + SDK refresh hook. For Dedicated/Private you can add mTLS. SDKs include a tokenProvider callback to fetch/refresh credentials.

Minimal examples (pseudo payloads)
- Chat request (single-turn, JSON):
  {
    \"model\":\"redwood-chat-1\",
    \"messages\":[{\"role\":\"system\",\"content\":\"You are a helpful support assistant.\"},{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"Issue: login fails with 403\"}],
    \"stream\": false,
    \"max_tokens\": 512,
    \"stop\":[\"\
--END-RESPONSE--\"]
  }

- Streaming (SSE) behavior (client receives events):
  event: delta
  data: {\"role\":\"assistant\",\"delta\":\"We recommend resetting your token...\"}

  event: delta
  data: {\"role\":\"assistant\",\"delta\":\"...then check the auth header.\"}

  event: done
  data: {\"finishedReason\":\"stop_sequence\"}

- Embeddings batch (JSON):
  POST /v1/embeddings
  {\"model\":\"redwood-embed-1024\",\"input\":[\"doc1 text\",\"doc2 text\"]}

Edge cases
- If you receive a done/reason:length, you can re-issue a chained request with last-n tokens as context for continuation. Our SDK has helpers for continuation tokens.
- When routing between Hosted and Dedicated, prefix cache keys with \"dedicated:<team>\" to avoid cache pollution.

Next actions from us
1) I will send a small SDK example (node + python) showing chat + streaming + tokenProvider.
2) Marissa will draft a sample reranker flow and estimated cost for hosted reranking vs local re-rank.
3) We’ll propose a follow-up call next week to walk your infra team through token minting.

Connor
Account Exec — Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.com

--
