From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
To: Ari Thompson <ari@lexicon.ai>
Cc: Priya Desai <priya@lexicon.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 16:28:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Lexicon demo — model fit and quality checks

Hi Ari — great to meet you (virtually) today, thanks for sending the prompt bank. Attached is a short evaluation pack I put together from the sample prompts you sent. Quick notes up front:

- Recommended models: rdx-medium-1 (good latency/cost balance) and rdx-large-q4f for stricter hallucination control on summarization.
- Benchmarks: I exported a small comparison run that includes Rouge-L, factuality checks (lexical overlap + model-based fact-checker), and token cost breakdown — link to Drive below.
- Prompt sets: I mapped your sample prompts to recommended templates and included a small gold set for regression monitoring (prompt_set_v1.jsonl).
- Regression mitigation: Redwood supports model pinning per-route and automated canary rollouts. If you detect a regression after a model update we can: 1) pin the route to the previous exact model build (immutable id), 2) open a priority ticket to route requests to dedicated fallback capacity while we investigate, or 3) apply a transient prompt shim (we can surface suggested prompt-level offsets). Pinning is generally the fastest remediation.

Attachments: lexicon_eval_summary_20260112.csv, prompt_set_v1.jsonl, pin_request_template.pdf
Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB_example_benchmarks
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/record/LEX-2026-07

Next steps I suggest:
1) Book the 60-min technical deep dive with Sean (solutions eng) — can you do Wed 1/14 or Thu 1/15?
2) I'll draft a pin-request playbook and a short runbook for regression triage for your SREs.
3) If legal needs a sample SLA language for rollback/incident windows, Monica can surface precedent language we use for Dedicated customers.

Happy to hop on a quick call to walk through the CSV results.

Cheers,
Hannah Schmitt
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference

title: Post-call: RAG qualification triage and next-step ask list (retrieval + reranker)
preview:
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Sara Lin <sara.lin@clearline.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 11 Jan 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: RAG qualification triage and next-step ask list (retrieval + reranker)

Sara — thanks again for taking time today. Quick digest of what we discussed and a proposed immediate plan so we can scope a short pilot (2–4 weeks):

Summary from call
- Goal: Reduce hallucination in patient-summary generation for provider-facing workflows while keeping latency < 450ms p50.
- Current infra: Clearline uses an internal vector DB (FAISS snapshot) and a semantic embedder (open-source embedder v0.7) with a lightweight cross-encoder reranker.
- Pain points: inconsistent grounding on recent policies, reranker adds variable latency, and occasional quality regressions after model updates.

Immediate qualification asks (what we need to get started)
1) A 3–4 document prompt-set that captures typical Q/A and two long-form summarization prompts (we can adapt; sample template attached).
2) A representative doc corpus slice (500–1,000 docs) we can index for a sanity pilot (S3 share or Drive link).
3) Clarify latency SLOs per route and a brief note on acceptable cost levers (e.g., willingness to accept quantized model variants).

Redwood suggestions (high level) — we'll follow up with Neha's technical note:
- Candidate encoder: text-embed-redwood-001 for stable cross-model embeddings (we saw better cross-run stability vs open-source embedder).
- Retrieval sweep: try top_k 30 with an initial filter by doc recency (3–12 months) and rerank top_k 10 with a fast cross-encoder.
- Reranker recommendation: cross-encoder-v2 (balanced latency/precision) then evaluate a distilled cross-attention reranker if latency is high.

Attachments: pilot scope template + prompt-set template + quick bench CSV stub.

Proposed next steps (we can turn around the first sanity run in ~5 business days once we have the corpus slice and prompt-set):
- Laura to send pilot scope (attached) and initial timeline.
- Neha to provide a short-run config (embedding + retrieval + reranker) and suggested bench script.
- Sara: share corpus slice + confirm procurement cadence.

Thanks — Sean

--
Sean Gallagher
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123
Redwood Inference


From: Sara Lin <sara.lin@clearline.ai>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@clearline.ai
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 09:42:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: RAG qualification triage and next-step ask list (retrieval + reranker)

Sean — thanks for the clear recap. Couple quick items from our side: we can provide a 700-doc slice (PHI redacted) and the two summarization prompts by EOD Tuesday. Procurement flagged a standard NDA first; I copied them here.

One question on the reranker: our current cross-encoder is custom and runs on CPU; what sort of latency hit should we expect if we swap in a Redwood reranker versus trying to optimize our current one?

I'll get the corpus link once procurement confirms NDA.

— Sara

On Sun, Jan 11, 2026 at 3:03 PM Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com> wrote:
> Sara — thanks again for taking time today. Quick digest of what we discussed and a proposed immediate plan so we can scope a short pilot (2–4 weeks):
> ...


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Sara Lin <sara.lin@clearline.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 13:07:00 -08:00
Subject: RE: Post-call: RAG qualification triage and next-step ask list (retrieval + reranker)

Adding the technical laundry list and an actionable pilot config based on today's notes. TL;DR: start with conservative retrieval (top_k 30) + rerank top 10 with cross-encoder-v2; measure grounding_precision@5 and hallucination_rate over prompt-set.

Pilot config (sanity run)
- Embedder: text-embed-redwood-001 (embedding dim 1536). Run a quick A/B vs your current embedder on 200 queries for embedding-stability and recall delta.
- Retriever: ANN (FAISS HNSW) with top_k_fetch = 30; apply doc-age filter window = 12 months (configurable).
- Reranker: cross-encoder-v2 (recommended) — initial model size medium (approx 1.5B). If p95 latency for reranker path > 300ms, fallback to distilled-cross-reranker v0.9.
- Post-process: aggressive answer grounding check — require that at least 2 retrieved docs contain overlapping entity spans with generated answer; otherwise mark as low-confidence and include source list.

Benchmarks to capture (we'll run and share CSV):
- Grounding_precision@5 (fraction answers where sources fully support claims)
- Recall@10 pre and post-rerank
- Hallucination_rate (manual spot-check on 100 queries)
- Latency p50/p95 and tokens-per-second cost per route

Prompt sets and evals
- Start with Sara's 2 summarization prompts + our 10 probe Qs (attachment: prompt-sets-clearline-v1.jsonl). We'll run 200 queries through each pipeline variant.

Quick results from internal sanity test (toy corpus, not your data)
- text-embed-redwood-001 vs open-embed v0.7: +6% recall@10, recall stability across runs improved (fewer swapped rank orders).
- cross-encoder-v2 vs lightweight cross-encoder: precision@5 +12pt, median reranker latency +120ms on CPU but ~35–60ms on a small GPU.

Files attached: prompt-sets-clearline-v1.jsonl (sample prompts), quick-bench-summary.csv (toy run).

Next actions (for pilot kickoff):
1) Clearline: share corpus slice & confirm NDA status.
2) Redwood: provision a small GPU sandbox with the recommended reranker so we can demonstrate latency/precision tradeoffs.
3) Procurement + AE: align on commercial pilot terms (Ben).

Happy to jump on a short tech call if your infra team wants to review reranker resource plans.

— Neha

Neha Kapoor
Sr. Applied ML Engineer, Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.com


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Sara Lin <sara.lin@clearline.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, procurement@clearline.ai
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 09:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: RAG qualification triage and next-step ask list (retrieval + reranker)

Perfect — thanks for the fast turnaround, Sara. Procurement's NDA is fine; Ben will pick up the commercial pilot terms and Laura will share a tightened 2-week pilot timeline (with an optional follow-on).

To keep momentum, proposed immediate schedule:
- Tue (today): Sara to share corpus slice link and final prompts.
- Wed: Redwood spins up sandbox + quick bench run (Neha leading). Expect initial bench summary by COB Wed.
- Fri: Short demo call (15–20m) to review initial grounding precision numbers and iterate.

If we get the corpus this afternoon, we'll prioritize the reranker-on-GPU run so we can show a practical latency delta vs CPU. If that looks good, we can scope a 2-week pilot that includes a small rollout and quality/regression guardrails (we'll propose alerts for hallucination_rate drift tied to prompt-set baselines).

Thanks — Sean

--
Sean Gallagher
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123
Redwood Inference

title: Routing + Control Plane readiness review (timeline to enable dry-run in prod)
preview:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Routing + Control Plane readiness review (timeline to enable dry-run in prod)

Team,

Ahead of next week’s controlled failover window, can we do a quick async readiness pass for routing + control plane so we can enable failover controller *dry-run* safely in prod (no policy flips) and start collecting “would-have-done” evidence?

Target:
- Dry-run enabled in prod by Wed 1/13 (scoped: Hosted API, limited routes/tenants)
- First production game day remains Thu 1/15 (with explicit go/no-go)

I put a checklist + current PR status in this doc:
- Attachment: Routing_ControlPlane_Readiness_Checklist_v4 (Google Doc link)

Here’s the condensed list so we can reply-all with green/yellow/red:

1) Policy evaluation determinism + signal priority
- Confirm deterministic ordering and tie-break behavior (no “random” flips under noisy signals)
- Required: signal priority matches ADR-042 (reachability > 5xx burn > tail latency burn > capacity)
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19812-policy-engine-signal-priority (status?)

2) Failover controller dry-run and auditability
- Dry-run emits recommended actions + evidence signals and writes structured audit event (who/what/why)
- No writes to routing policy stores in dry-run mode
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19814-failover-controller-dry-run (status?)
  - redwood/pr-19824-failover-event-audit-log (status?)

3) Rollback/anti-flap guardrails (even if dry-run)
- We need the config schema + enforcement ready so when we later flip from dry-run -> execute, we’re not “re-learning” safety constraints
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19815-routing-policy-rollback-safety
  - redwood/pr-19833-failover-controller-rate-limits

4) Control plane / routing dependencies
- Region health service endpoint availability + TTL / caching to avoid stampedes during partial outages
- Routing reads region health in a bounded way (timeouts/fallback)
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19810-add-region-health-aggregator-service
  - redwood/pr-19825-region-health-cache-ttl

5) Observability + alerting to support dry-run validation
- Metrics for: dry-run recommendations, trigger source, failover_reason (low-cardinality), confidence/corroboration count
- Burn-rate rules exist but are tuned to avoid paging noise during the dry-run period
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19816-add-multi-region-slo-metrics
  - redwood/pr-19818-alerts-failover-burn-rate
  - observability-pack/pr-907-failover-dashboards-and-alert-rules

6) Gateway + client behavior considerations (so dry-run evidence matches reality)
- Timeouts/retry budgets tuned to avoid masking regional impairment / amplifying spikes
- PR:
  - redwood/pr-19820-api-gateway-fallback-timeout-tuning

7) Infra / DNS routing prerequisites (for later execution / fast detection)
- Confirm DNS TTL plan + rollout guardrails are approved; no change required for dry-run but want us aligned
- PR:
  - redwood-terraform/pr-1442-dns-ttl-and-failover-routing-updates

If each of you can reply with:
- ✅ ready / ⚠️ at risk / 🛑 blocked
- what is needed + date you expect to clear

…we can lock the Wed enablement plan and avoid a last-minute scramble.

Thanks,
Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
Program Operations (Reliability)
Redwood Inference



From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Routing + Control Plane readiness review (timeline to enable dry-run in prod)

✅ from SRE side *for dry-run enablement* assuming the scope stays “Hosted API / limited tenants” and we have two items in place:

1) Dashboards annotated by the controller (even in dry-run)
- We need an explicit marker when a dry-run recommendation would have triggered.
- Otherwise reviewing after the fact becomes guesswork (we already got burned on this in rehearsal).

2) Clear success criteria for dry-run period
- I suggest we define: false-positive rate threshold, median time-to-recommend, and “corroboration present” %.
- We can turn these into an internal weekly note before we allow execute mode.

PR status from what I last saw:
- pr-19818 burn alerts: close, but we should reduce paging route on first rollout (page only when reachability + 5xx corroborate; tail latency alone -> ticket).
- observability-pack/pr-907: ready for staging; prod deployment gating on label cardinality review.

Also requesting:
- incident-bot summary (pr-412) should go out for dry-run recs to #eng-sre (no paging). That’s the easiest way for on-call to build trust.

I’ll sync w/ Allison/Sergio separately, but we’re not blocked for Wed if we keep the blast radius tight.

Rafael
SRE Manager, Redwood Inference



From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>

text:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Routing + Control Plane readiness review (timeline to enable dry-run in prod)

Team,

Ahead of next week’s controlled failover window, can we do a quick async readiness pass for routing + control plane so we can enable failover controller *dry-run* safely in prod (no policy flips) and start collecting “would-have-done” evidence?

Target:
- Dry-run enabled in prod by Wed 1/13 (scoped: Hosted API, limited routes/tenants)
- First production game day remains Thu 1/15 (with explicit go/no-go)

I put a checklist + current PR status in this doc:
- Attachment: Routing_ControlPlane_Readiness_Checklist_v4 (Google Doc link)

Here’s the condensed list so we can reply-all with green/yellow/red:

1) Policy evaluation determinism + signal priority
- Confirm deterministic ordering and tie-break behavior (no “random” flips under noisy signals)
- Required: signal priority matches ADR-042 (reachability > 5xx burn > tail latency burn > capacity)
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19812-policy-engine-signal-priority (status?)

2) Failover controller dry-run and auditability
- Dry-run emits recommended actions + evidence signals and writes structured audit event (who/what/why)
- No writes to routing policy stores in dry-run mode
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19814-failover-controller-dry-run (status?)
  - redwood/pr-19824-failover-event-audit-log (status?)

3) Rollback/anti-flap guardrails (even if dry-run)
- We need the config schema + enforcement ready so when we later flip from dry-run -> execute, we’re not “re-learning” safety constraints
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19815-routing-policy-rollback-safety
  - redwood/pr-19833-failover-controller-rate-limits

4) Control plane / routing dependencies
- Region health service endpoint availability + TTL / caching to avoid stampedes during partial outages
- Routing reads region health in a bounded way (timeouts/fallback)
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19810-add-region-health-aggregator-service
  - redwood/pr-19825-region-health-cache-ttl

5) Observability + alerting to support dry-run validation
- Metrics for: dry-run recommendations, trigger source, failover_reason (low-cardinality), confidence/corroboration count
- Burn-rate rules exist but are tuned to avoid paging noise during the dry-run period
- PRs:
  - redwood/pr-19816-add-multi-region-slo-metrics
  - redwood/pr-19818-alerts-failover-burn-rate
  - observability-pack/pr-907-failover-dashboards-and-alert-rules

6) Gateway + client behavior considerations (so dry-run evidence matches reality)
- Timeouts/retry budgets tuned to avoid masking regional impairment / amplifying spikes
- PR:
  - redwood/pr-19820-api-gateway-fallback-timeout-tuning

7) Infra / DNS routing prerequisites (for later execution / fast detection)
- Confirm DNS TTL plan + rollout guardrails are approved; no change required for dry-run but want us aligned
- PR:
  - redwood-terraform/pr-1442-dns-ttl-and-failover-routing-updates

If each of you can reply with:
- ✅ ready / ⚠️ at risk / 🛑 blocked
- what is needed + date you expect to clear

…we can lock the Wed enablement plan and avoid a last-minute scramble.

Thanks,
Priya

—
Priya Natarajan
Program Operations (Reliability)
Redwood Inference



From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 10, 2026 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Routing + Control Plane readiness review (timeline to enable dry-run in prod)

✅ from SRE side *for dry-run enablement* assuming the scope stays “Hosted API / limited tenants” and we have two items in place:

1) Dashboards annotated by the controller (even in dry-run)
- We need an explicit marker when a dry-run recommendation would have triggered.
- Otherwise reviewing after the fact becomes guesswork (we already got burned on this in rehearsal).

2) Clear success criteria for dry-run period
- I suggest we define: false-positive rate threshold, median time-to-recommend, and “corroboration present” %.
- We can turn these into an internal weekly note before we allow execute mode.

PR status from what I last saw:
- pr-19818 burn alerts: close, but we should reduce paging route on first rollout (page only when reachability + 5xx corroborate; tail latency alone -> ticket).
- observability-pack/pr-907: ready for staging; prod deployment gating on label cardinality review.

Also requesting:
- incident-bot summary (pr-412) should go out for dry-run recs to #eng-sre (no paging). That’s the easiest way for on-call to build trust.

I’ll sync w/ Allison/Sergio separately, but we’re not blocked for Wed if we keep the blast radius tight.

Rafael
SRE Manager, Redwood Inference

As a next step for our security review, can you please complete the attached vendor security questionnaire and share the following artifacts:

Hannah

2) Authentication + access controls (SSO/SAML, RBAC)
3) Audit logs (coverage, access, export, retention)
4) Encryption + key management (at rest/in transit; KMS options in private)
5) HIPAA / BAA process + next steps

Sean will join to cover logging/retention/export patterns and reliability-related questions.

See you Wed,
Hannah

Hannah Schmitt
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
(Email is best)

title: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Sean — requesting SRE + exec approval to enable the mixed-workload scheduler prototype behind flag in a *limited* production canary (Hosted API, us-east-1).

Why now
- We have repeat internal + customer escalations where mixed short/long prompts inflate p95/TTFT (HOL blocking during long prefill).
- The runtime team has a prototype (two-queue short/long + aging + token budget guardrails) that shows consistent p95 improvement on mixed distributions without material throughput loss in our benchmark suite.

What we’re proposing (scope + blast radius)
- Region/pool: Hosted API us-east-1, pool: hosted-canary (not general).
- Traffic: 1% -> 5% (same pool) over ~3 hours if metrics stay within guardrails.
- Duration: 24 hours at 5% (or revert sooner if drift).
- Feature gating:
  - Flag: scheduler.mixed_workload.enabled=true (pool-scoped)
  - Policy knobs pinned (no auto-tuning) for the canary window.

Success criteria (must hit)
- Mixed workload p95 latency (end-to-end) improves >= 15% vs baseline (comparable traffic band), measured on canary + perf-canary.
- No p95 regression > 5% on steady-state short-only workload.
- No error-rate increase beyond normal variance; no sustained queue starvation counters.

Guardrails / rollback triggers (aligned to SRE doc)
- Rollback immediately if any of:
  - p95 (Hosted API chat) +10% for > 10 minutes vs same-hour baseline
  - p99 +15% for > 10 minutes
  - starvation_long_counter increments continuously for > 2 minutes (indicates long queue not making progress)
  - elevated OOM/compute errors attributable to batch construction changes
- Rollback options:
  1) Flip flag off (fast, no deploy) — expected to drain within minutes.
  2) Revert knobs to baseline (if only parameter-induced).
  3) Runtime rollback to 1.17.3 in hosted-canary (Connor has the release playbook ready).

Known risks (and mitigations)
- Risk: long-queue starvation under sustained short traffic
  - Mitigation: aging + min dispatch share + regression test merged (PR #24905).
- Risk: kernel selection instability when alternating short/long sequences
  - Mitigation: prefill/decode separation hooks + capped token budget per iteration; monitored via kernel_thrash metric + Nsight spot checks in staging.
- Risk: throughput dip due to policy overhead
  - Mitigation: overhead measured <1% CPU in staging; monitor tokens/sec and batch utilization.

Links / artifacts
- Results summary (attached): mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf
- Canary plan draft (attached): canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx
- Confluence design + ADR:
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SERVE/Mixed-workload+scheduler+prototype+-+Design
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-Short-Long-Prompt-Scheduling-Policy
- Perf-canary dashboard:
  - https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-mixed/mixed-workload-perf-canary?var-runtime=1.18.0-rc3

Request
- Sean: please reply with SRE sign-off (or required edits) so Connor can schedule the window today.
- If approved, we’ll do the ramp during US business hours with Rafael as ops point and Aisha watching SLO + rollback triggers.

Thanks,
Mateo
Executive Sponsor, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachment: mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf (application/pdf)
Attachment: canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

A couple asks before I sign off:

1) Can you confirm the change is *pool-scoped only* (no spillover to other hosted pools via shared config)?
2) What’s our exact dashboard + alert set for queue starvation + per-class waiting time? I don’t want this to be “we’ll eyeball it.”
3) In the results summary: what’s the worst-case scenario you saw (any regressions on long-only or long-spike mixes)?

If we can nail those three, I’m comfortable with a 1% -> 5% ramp with a tight rollback.

-Sean

---

From: Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:36 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Responding inline:

> 1) confirm pool-scoped only
Yes. The flag + knobs are resolved at pool config level (runtime config service), and we’re only enabling for pool=hosted-canary in us-east-1. No global default change. (Config diff is attached in the canary plan doc; Rafael can also paste the exact key path if helpful.)

> 2) dashboard + alert set
Allison has the dashboard spec + alerts wired. We’ll be monitoring:
- scheduler_queue_depth{class=short|long}
- scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class=short|long}
- scheduler_starvation_events_total{class=long}
- scheduler_dispatch_share{class=short|long}
- plus end-to-end p95/p99 and TTFT.

> 3) worst-case scenario seen
In “long-spike” synthetic (10 minutes of 70% long-context), we saw:
- Mixed p95 improved still (less HOL for short),
- Long-class p95 increased ~3–6% depending on SKU, but no starvation due to aging + min-share.
We did see one starvation corner case early (continuous short flood); that’s what PR #24905 addresses, and it’s in the 1.18.0-rc3 build proposed.

Relevant PRs:
- Two-queue policy: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24844
- Metrics: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24888
- Starvation fix: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24905

-Lena

---

From: Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 11:05 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Dashboard is live for hosted-canary / us-east-1:
https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/sched-class/scheduler-class-metrics?var-region=us-east-1&var-pool=hosted-canary

Alerts (PagerDuty: Runtime-SRE)
- “SchedulerLongClassStarvation”
  - fires if scheduler_starvation_events_total{class="long"} increases AND scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class="long"} > 20000ms for 2m
- “SchedulerClassSkewTooHigh”
  - fires if dispatch_share{class="short"} > 0.95 for 5m (indicates long not getting scheduled)
- “SchedulerQueueTimeRegressionShort”
  - fires if time_in_queue p95 short > baseline + 15% for 10m

These are documented in the dashboard “Readme” panel and mirror the Hosted API tail latency guardrails doc.

-Allison

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 11:42 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Approved from SRE side with the following conditions:
- Ramp stays within the guardrails described (1% -> 5% only; no additional pools without a follow-up).
- Aisha or Rafael is explicitly watching during the ramp window; if alerts fire, rollback is immediate (no “let’s wait it out”).
- Connor to confirm rollback path (flag off + runtime rollback) is ready before we start.

Thanks for putting the monitoring in place.

-Sean

---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 12:06 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Confirmed — we’ll follow those conditions.

Connor: please schedule the ramp for this afternoon (target start 2:00pm PT / 5:00pm ET) and reply-all with the exact change window + rollback checklist.

Rafael/Aisha: you’re on point for the ramp. If any of the three rollback triggers hit (p95/p99 drift, starvation, error-rate anomalies), roll back immediately and we’ll do the postmortem tomorrow.

Thanks all.

-Mateo

title: Intermittent inference jitter impacting a subset of customers
preview:
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Nimbus Retail Ops <ops@nimbusretail.com>; Anya Patel <anya@nimbusretail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Intermittent inference jitter impacting a subset of customers

Hello,

We wanted to let you know we are investigating intermittent increases in token-level latency (jitter) for a small percentage of hosted inference requests affecting endpoints in us-west-1. Early impact window: 07:50-08:00 PST. Symptoms: short, sporadic increases in p95/p99 (generation stall) and rare truncated responses.

What we're doing:
- Ops has opened an incident on the status page (https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20251201-jitter)
- Roll-forward of a routing policy to reduce fanout for affected pools
- Live tracing enabled for impacted customer tokens and KV cache health checks

Customer impact: intermittent slow tails (p95 up to ~1.5x baseline) and a handful of truncated responses. No data loss reported. If you observed anything different, please reply with example request ids.

Expected next updates: 30 mins or sooner if we have substantive findings. We're prioritizing full post-incident analysis and will share a short postmortem once the incident is closed.

Attachments: incident_snapshot_20251201.json (server-side traces, truncated)

Thanks,
Irene Choi
Sr. Site Reliability Engineer
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment stubs:
- incident_snapshot_20251201.json (trace snippet, 28 KB)

From: Anya Patel <anya@nimbusretail.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 08:26:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Intermittent inference jitter impacting a subset of customers

Thanks for the heads up.

We saw a couple of elevated user-facing errors this morning (around 07:52 PST) that caused timeouts in our live checkout flow. Can you confirm whether any retries could have led to duplicated prompts? Our compliance team also requests an ETA for the postmortem and whether there will be any credits for time impacted — this week is a high-traffic window for us.

Example request id: nimbus-req-20251201-7751

-Anya


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Intermittent inference jitter impacting a subset of customers

Folks — forwarding Anya's reply and Irene's initial notice. Suggested customer response below.

Key asks from Nimbus:
- Confirm whether retries may have produced duplicate prompts
- ETA for postmortem
- Credit/comp accommodation for impact window

Proposed next steps:
1) Marissa: lead the customer-facing postmortem invite (30-min call) and confirm any duplicated request behavior from logs.
2) Sean: pull request-level retry traces for nimbus-req-20251201-7751 and any correlated spans. If duplication occurred, flag for credit.
3) Irene: produce an internal incident notes doc and link to postmortem draft.

I'm attaching a short credit proposal draft to share if duplication/timeout-induced errors are confirmed.

Attachments: credit_proposal_nimbus_v1.docx

Karthik

-----Original message-----
On Mon, 01 Dec 2025 08:12:00 -0700 Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hello,
> 
> We wanted to let you know we are investigating intermittent increases in token-level latency (jitter) for a small percentage of hosted inference requests affecting endpoints in us-west-1. Early impact window: 07:50-08:00 PST. Symptoms: short, sporadic increases in p95/p99 (generation stall) and rare truncated responses.
> 
> What we're doing:
> - Ops has opened an incident on the status page (https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20251201-jitter)
> - Roll-forward of a routing policy to reduce fanout for affected pools
> - Live tracing enabled for impacted customer tokens and KV cache health checks
> 
> Customer impact: intermittent slow tails (p95 up to ~1.5x baseline) and a handful of truncated responses. No data loss reported. If you observed anything different, please reply with example request ids.
> 
> Expected next updates: 30 mins or sooner if we have substantive findings. We're prioritizing full post-incident analysis and will share a short postmortem once the incident is closed.
>

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Anya Patel <anya@nimbusretail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 13:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intermittent inference jitter impacting a subset of customers

Hi Anya — thanks for raising the request id and flagging the customer impact. We completed a short triage and here are the findings and next steps.

Summary of findings (short):
- Root cause: transient KV cache thrash in the us-west-1 inference pool caused an amplification of disk-backed cache lookups during a brief traffic spike; this increased per-token latency for affected requests.
- Duplication: We did not observe duplicate prompt replays from the service side; retries from your client were attempted and may have contributed to user-facing timeouts. Our traces show a retry from the SDK at 07:53:12 for nimbus-req-20251201-7751.

Action taken:
- Adjusted routing fanout and disabled a speculative prefetch for the impacted pool at 08:03 PST.
- Increased KV cache eviction thresholds and rolled a runtime patch to reduce disk fallback for high-throughput prefixes.
- Added extra health checks to detect cache-pressure earlier.

Customer accommodations:
- We will apply a one-off credit for the affected window (2025-12-01 07:50–08:10 PST). Proposed credit amount and paperwork attached; happy to adjust after a brief call.
- If you'd like us to join a quick postmortem review, we can do a 30-minute call today or tomorrow.

Full postmortem draft: https://drive.google.com/d/1aBExamplePostmortem (internal view shared via expiring link)
Status page incident: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20251201-jitter

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Nimbus Retail Ops <ops@nimbusretail.com>; Anya Patel <anya@nimbusretail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Intermittent inference jitter impacting a subset of customers

Hello,

We wanted to let you know we are investigating intermittent increases in token-level latency (jitter) for a small percentage of hosted inference requests affecting endpoints in us-west-1. Early impact window: 07:50-08:00 PST. Symptoms: short, sporadic increases in p95/p99 (generation stall) and rare truncated responses.

What we're doing:
- Ops has opened an incident on the status page (https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20251201-jitter)
- Roll-forward of a routing policy to reduce fanout for affected pools
- Live tracing enabled for impacted customer tokens and KV cache health checks

Customer impact: intermittent slow tails (p95 up to ~1.5x baseline) and a handful of truncated responses. No data loss reported. If you observed anything different, please reply with example request ids.

Expected next updates: 30 mins or sooner if we have substantive findings. We're prioritizing full post-incident analysis and will share a short postmortem once the incident is closed.

Attachments: incident_snapshot_20251201.json (server-side traces, truncated)

Thanks,
Irene Choi
Sr. Site Reliability Engineer
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Anya Patel <anya@nimbusretail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 13:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intermittent inference jitter impacting a subset of customers

Hi Anya — thanks for raising the request id and flagging the customer impact. We completed a short triage and here are the findings and next steps.

Summary of findings (short):
- Root cause: transient KV cache thrash in the us-west-1 inference pool caused an amplification of disk-backed cache lookups during a brief traffic spike; this increased per-token latency for affected requests.
- Duplication: We did not observe duplicate prompt replays from the service side; retries from your client were attempted and may have contributed to user-facing timeouts. Our traces show a retry from the SDK at 07:53:12 for nimbus-req-20251201-7751.

Action taken:
- Adjusted routing fanout and disabled a speculative prefetch for the impacted pool at 08:03 PST.
- Increased KV cache eviction thresholds and rolled a runtime patch to reduce disk fallback for high-throughput prefixes.
- Added extra health checks to detect cache-pressure earlier.

Customer accommodations:
- We will apply a one-off credit for the affected window (2025-12-01 07:50–08:10 PST). Proposed credit amount and paperwork attached; happy to adjust after a brief call.
- If you'd like us to join a quick postmortem review, we can do a 30-minute call today or tomorrow.

Full postmortem draft: https://drive.google.com/d/1aBExamplePostmortem (internal view shared via expiring link)
Status page incident: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20251201-jitter

Attachments: nimbus_postmortem_draft_20251201.pdf, credit_proposal_nimbus_v1.docx

Apologies again for the disruption. We'll follow up with a finalized root cause postmortem and timeline by EOD tomorrow.

title: POC planning for BroadTech – ownership, timeline, and blocker log
preview:
From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Park <epark@broadtech.ai>, Aaron Liu <aaron.liu@broadtech.ai>
Cc: Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-18 14:02 PST
Subject: POC planning: next steps and proposed owners

Thanks again for taking the call today — good discussion and lots of alignment. Per request, I pulled together a compact recap with proposed success criteria, timeline, and owners so we have a single place to iterate. I attached a POC plan workbook (redwood-poc-plan-v1.xlsx) and a short doc with the success criteria template.

High-level summary (from today's call):
- Objective: Evaluate Redwood Inference for low-latency, cost-predictable LLM inference for BroadTech's in-product assistant (targeting sequence lengths ~512).
- Primary success criteria (draft):
  1) 95th pct latency <= 220ms for single-query traffic at 50 RPS (baseline workload).
  2) End-to-end cost per 1M tokens <= $X (we'll refine after initial perf runs).
  3) Quality parity: no >10% drop vs your current model on the 50-sample regression suite (we'll get your eval set).
  4) Data control: all inference logs remain within BroadTech VPC during trial (required).

Proposed POC timeline (subject to your confirmation):
- Week 0 (Nov 24): kickoff + onboarding; exchange eval dataset and security checklist.
- Week 1 (Nov 25-Dec 1): infra/setup + test deployment; first perf baseline.
- Week 2 (Dec 2-8): tuned runs, cost profiling, quality evals.
- Week 3 (Dec 9-15): joint review; decision/next action.

Ownership (suggested):
- BroadTech: Emily (Product) — final sign-off on success criteria; Aaron (AI) — provides eval dataset and failure cases.
- Redwood: Diego (AE) — overall coordination; Maya (Technical Solutions) — POC runbook and mitigations; Rachel (Security) — questionnaire and evidence.

Immediate asks / next steps: please confirm the owners above and attach your evaluation set (anonymized) and the security point-of-contact. If any of the success criteria above are off, flag now — we'll finalize before kickoff.

Thanks,
Diego Fernandez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
D: +1 (415) 555-0192
attachments: redwood-poc-plan-v1.xlsx, broadtech-success-criteria.docx


From: Emily Park <epark@broadtech.ai>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aaron Liu <aaron.liu@broadtech.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-18 16:37 PST
Subject: Re: POC planning: next steps and proposed owners

Diego — thanks for the clear recap. High level looks good; a few clarifications and constraints from our side:

Clarifications / edits to success criteria:
- Latency: the 95th <=220ms target is aggressive but reasonable if the model variant is comparable. Can we scope an initial baseline run with our small test audience (10 RPS) first and then scale?
- Cost target: we don't have an exact $X number yet — our procurement asks that Redwood provide a same-workload cost estimate after Week 1 runs.
- Quality parity: we'll provide a 100-sample eval set (anonymized) and a list of 15 failure cases (customer privacy redactions included).
- Data control: mandatory. We need confirmation that logs will not leave our VPC and any retained audit logs are available for 30 days.

Constraints:
- We are under a hiring freeze for infra resources; don't assume we can provide engineers beyond Aaron and a single SRE (TBD).
- Security: SOC2 Type II evidence required before any host-handover; Rachel, can you advise on the minimum evidence packet you need?

We can do the kickoff on Nov 24 AM Pacific. Owners confirmed: Emily (Product), Aaron (AI). I will upload the eval set to the Drive link you shared by EOD Nov 19.

—Emily
Emily Park
Sr. Product Manager, BroadTech
attachments: broadtech-evalset-100.zip (uploaded to Drive)


From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Park <epark@broadtech.ai>, Aaron Liu <aaron.liu@broadtech.ai>
Cc: Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-19 09:08 PST
Subject: Re: POC planning: next steps and proposed owners

Perfect — thanks Emily. I've updated the attached workbook with a phased scaling plan (Phase A: 10 RPS baseline run; Phase B: scale to 50 RPS if Phase A passes). Key updates in the workbook: acceptance gates, runbook steps, and a short blocker escalation matrix.

Quick notes on the security ask: Rachel — based on BroadTech's request for SOC2 evidence, can we compile a minimal packet (SOC2 attestation copy, retention policy excerpt, access control summary) that we can share under NDA before Nov 24? I'd like to avoid gating the kickoff if possible.

Updated timeline (compact):
- Nov 19: Redwood uploads instance configs + pre-built container images to private Drive link.
- Nov 24: Kickoff + runbook walkthrough (30–45m).
- Nov 25–27: Phase A perf runs (10 RPS) + first quality eval.
- Dec 1–5: Phase B scaling & cost profiling (if Phase A passes).

I'm tracking blockers in the workbook but starting a short live blocker log below (will flesh out in shared sheet):
- BLOCKER-1: Eval data format inconsistent (customer) — mitigation: Redwood will provide a small ingest script and template by Nov 20. Owner: Diego / Aaron.
- BLOCKER-2: SOC2 evidence hold (security) — mitigation: Rachel to send minimal evidence; Redwood will accept provisional view under NDA. Owner: Rachel.
- BLOCKER-3: KV cache warm-up causing elevated p95 on first runs — mitigation: Maya to enable warm-up sequences and pre-warm cache; add 30m warm-up window before measurement. Owner: Maya.

Please confirm if the mitigations/owners above look right. If no objections I'll schedule the kickoff for Nov 24 10:00am PT and share a calendar invite.

Diego Fernandez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood-poc-plan-v1.xlsx


From: Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.ai>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emily Park <epark@broadtech.ai>, Aaron Liu <aaron.liu@broadtech.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-19 11:46 PST
Subject: Re: POC planning: next steps and proposed owners

Adding the technical risk log and escalation path from my side — pulled from the runbook and expanded with short mitigation steps. I'll also paste this into the shared workbook under the 'Blocker Log' tab so we have a single source of truth.

POC Risk / Blocker Log (initial):
- RISK-101 (Data ingest mismatch) — Symptoms: run fails during tokenization; impact: test can't start.
  Mitigation: small ingest utility (+ example CSV/JSON) provided by 11/20; fallback: Redwood uses synthetic masked data for initial perf tests.
  Escalation: Diego -> Maya -> Sean (Sales Eng lead) -> BroadTech escalation contact (Emily).

- RISK-102 (Latency spike due to cold KV cache) — Symptoms: p95 elevated for first 5–10 minutes.
  Mitigation: include 30m warm-up traffic and an explicit pre-warm endpoint; instrument to exclude warm-up window from measurement.
  Escalation: Maya -> Redwood SRE (pager duty) -> BroadTech SRE (if unresolved).

- RISK-103 (Quality regression on specific prompt types) — Symptoms: >10% drop on regression suite.
  Mitigation: preserve candidate prompts, roll back to earlier model variant, run targeted tuning.
  Escalation: Maya -> Rachel (if data handling issue suspected) -> Diego (contract/next steps).

Operational escalation contacts:
- Redwood on-call SRE: oncall@redwood.ai (PagerDuty)
- Redwood engineering escalation: seán.gallagher@redwood.ai (primary) [note: accent removed, please use sean.gallagher@redwood.ai]
- BroadTech escalation contact: Emily Park <epark@broadtech.ai>

I'll also prepare a short runbook play video (5–7 min) that walks through pre-warm and measurement steps; should have that uploaded by EOD Nov 20.

—Maya Srinivasan
Technical Solutions Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Emily Park <epark@broadtech.ai>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aaron Liu <aaron.liu@broadtech.ai>
Date: 2025-11-20 09:22 PST
Subject: Re: POC planning: next steps and proposed owners

Thanks everyone — confirmed. A few closing notes before kickoff:

1) We accept the phased plan (Phase A 10 RPS -> Phase B scale) and the owners you've listed.
2) I've uploaded the anonymized 100-sample eval set to your Drive link (see HubSpot/Drive links in thread).
3) On SOC2: I need Rachel's minimal evidence packet by EOD Nov 21 so Procurement can provision access to the VPC test environment.
4) Blockers: noted BLOCKER-1 and BLOCKER-3 — Aaron will attempt to have one SRE available remotely during Phase A but no guaranteed on-site time.

Please send the calendar invite for Nov 24 10:00am PT and I'll create a short agenda item list for the kickoff (owners + measurement definitions). Looking forward to it.

Emily Park
Sr. Product Manager, BroadTech

title: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility
preview:
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

Priya / Neha,

We’re kicking off the Provision-to-Ready automation sprint for Dedicated capacity + shared fleet bring-up. I want to align on (a) what “success” means in exec terms, and (b) a lightweight weekly reporting cadence so we don’t have to reconstruct this ad hoc during rollout.

Why now (exec framing)
- Dedicated expansions have been gated by manual steps and inconsistent node readiness. Time-to-ready is a top contributor to “capacity available vs capacity allocated” deltas.
- This sprint is intended to make bring-up repeatable across regions/GPU types via: golden images + automated orchestration + validated readiness gates.

Proposal: success metrics (what I’ll report weekly)
1) Time-to-ready (TTReady) for new nodes
   - P50 and P95 (overall + segmented by region + GPU type)
   - Definition: allocated -> Ready (with clear source-of-truth timestamps)
2) Provision-to-ready success rate
   - % nodes reaching Ready without human intervention within 24h
3) Validation gate health
   - Failure rate by step (GPU health/driver parity/kube join/network/storage/NCCL smoke/perf canary)
   - Quarantine count + top quarantine reasons
4) Rollout coverage
   - Regions/GPU types enabled behind flags, plus current constraints

Cadence
- Weekly update email every Friday EOD PT (one screen of bullets + a link to dashboard)
- Mid-week ping only if we hit stop conditions or need approval for staged rollout expansion

Ask
- Please confirm (or tweak) the metrics above and who should be on the weekly CC for exec visibility.
- If you have a strong preference on target thresholds (e.g., TTReady P95), call it out; otherwise we’ll report deltas from baseline and lock targets in week 1 once measurement is fully consistent.

Attaching a draft one-pager + baseline snapshot.

Attachments:
- Provision-to-Ready Sprint One-Pager v1.pdf
- Provision-to-Ready Baseline Metrics (Sep 2025).xlsx

Thanks,
Irene
—
Irene Choi
Director, Engineering Program Management
Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 9:34 AM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

Aligned on the framing and the reporting approach.

Two additions I’d like to see captured explicitly in the weekly update:
- Control-plane “readiness state machine” adoption: % of new nodes flowing through Allocated -> Provisioning -> Validating -> Ready via the new service (vs legacy/manual). This is the leading indicator that the platform integration is real.
- Safety/rollback: any time we have to flip the kill switch or roll back an image channel, call it out (even if customer impact is avoided). We should treat those as learning moments.

On targets: I’d rather we set them after Week 1 once the timestamps are defensible. The only non-negotiable is we don’t ship gates that create silent capacity loss (i.e., Ready is accurate, Quarantine is actionable).

Priya
—
Priya Natarajan
VP, Platform Engineering
Redwood Inference


From: Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

+1 to Priya.

A couple of exec-level clarifications I’ll want baked into the weekly note:
- What does “Ready” mean operationally? (e.g., eligible to join serving pool vs only joined cluster). If we change that definition mid-sprint, flag it.
- Customer impact indicator: is Dedicated expansion currently blocked, at risk, or on-track? (simple R/Y/G is fine)

Target-wise: directionally, we should be able to say “we reduced time-to-ready by X%” and “we reduced manual touches by Y%” by end of sprint. Don’t over-fit to absolute numbers in week one.

Re: CC list, keep it tight. I’d suggest just me + Priya + Sean; add others only when we’re asking for a go/no-go.

Neha


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 11:16 AM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

Got it. I’ll incorporate the additions and keep the CC tight (Priya/Neha/Sean + me; Vanessa only if there’s an approval ask or if we’re calling out a major operational decision).

Re: “Ready” definition — proposal for the weekly note (and we’ll pin this in the sprint page):
- Ready = node has:
  1) joined the cluster
  2) passed post-flight validations (GPU health + driver/toolkit parity + basic network/storage checks + NCCL smoke)
  3) passed a lightweight runtime/perf canary
  4) been marked Ready in the control-plane readiness service
  5) is eligible to be added to serving pools (subject to capacity policy)

If/when we decouple “Ready” from “eligible to serve,” I’ll call it out explicitly.

Weekly email template (one screen)
- Summary: R/Y/G for Dedicated expansion readiness
- TTReady: P50/P95 + delta vs baseline
- Manual touches: touches/node + top causes
- State machine adoption: % via new path
- Top 3 failure modes: step + failure rate + what we’re doing
- Safety events: kill switch / rollback / quarantine spike
- Next week: scope + decision points (if any)

I’ll also include links to:
- Sprint Confluence page
- Grafana dashboard
- Key Linear epics/tickets

If you’re OK with this, I’ll send the first weekly on Fri 10/3 with baseline + Week 1 plan, then start reporting actual metrics once we have the canonical timestamps finalized.

Irene


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 12:08 PM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

SRE POV: the template is good.

One suggestion: when you report TTReady, please include a “data quality” line for the first two weeks (e.g., whether allocated timestamp comes from cloud API vs internal allocator). We’ve had past confusion where the metric moved because the definition changed.

Also, for alerting/noise management during rollout, it would be helpful to track:
- flake rate on validations (NCCL + perf canary)
- quarantine escape hatch usage (operator overrides)

No further asks. Keep the CC list tight as discussed.

Sean
—
Sean Gallagher
Director, Site Reliability Engineering
Redwood Inference


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Gupta <neha.gupta@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 1:38 PM PT
Subject: Re: Provision-to-Ready automation sprint — kickoff, success metrics, and weekly exec visibility

Makes sense — I’ll add a “Metric definition / data quality” line item until we’re confident the allocated->ready timestamps are stable.

Plan:
- I’ll send the first update tomorrow (Fri 10/3) using the template + baseline + Week 1 measurement decisions.
- Subsequent weekly updates on Fridays EOD PT.
- Any kill switch / rollback / capacity-at-risk event will trigger a same-day heads up email (separately from the weekly).

Thanks all.

Irene

title: Request: SOC 2 Type II report onboarding — kickoff & templates
preview:
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rachel Lee <rlee@acmeanalytics.com>
Cc: security@acmeanalytics.com
Date: 2025-09-03T14:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request: SOC 2 Type II report onboarding — kickoff & templates

Hi Rachel,

Thanks again for the time today. As discussed, Acme will need our latest SOC 2 Type II report and a short bridge letter that clarifies the audit period and any in-flight control changes since the report's end date. To keep your procurement/compliance team moving, can you confirm the following and we'll assemble the package: 

- Desired audit period coverage (e.g., 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31)
- Formal contact for secure transfer (SFTP/Drive email)

title: NorthBridge demo — decision signal and next-step options
preview:
From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Connell <liam.oconnell@northbridge.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Jul 2025 14:12:00 -0700
Subject: NorthBridge demo — quick recap & questions

Thanks for taking the time today, Liam — appreciated the thoughtful questions. Quick recap of what we covered and two small asks so we can make next steps as low-effort as possible: 

1) Recap (short):
- Goal you shared: reduce per-query inference cost by ~40% while keeping 95th percentile latency under 150ms for your search rerank workload.
- Proposed approach: Redwood dedicated inference with KV-cache + intelligent batching and a mixed-precision quantized model variant.
- Main unknowns: exact request size distribution (avg tokens) and any hard constraints for PII handling / retention windows.

2) Asks (two quick things):
- Can you share a sample token histogram (or approximate avg tokens per request)? Even a ballpark helps us size the Dedicated pool and pick a quantization profile.
- Would you be open to a short 15-minute call next week to walk procurement through packaging and an initial estimate? If helpful, I can propose two options: a conservative 2-GPU pilot or a 6-week evaluation with autoscaling.

Attached the slide deck from the demo and a one‑page pricing snapshot for the two options.

Thanks — if you prefer, feel free to reply with a single number for average input tokens and I’ll take it from there.

Best,
Grace

Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0128

title: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)
preview:
From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Team,

We need to lock a plan for the integration test harness that creates/destroys an ephemeral VPC (ENG-4860) and how we fund + isolate it. Right now the proposal in the PR is “run on every PR,” which I don’t think we can sustain.

Can you reply with:
1) Estimated runtime + $/run (rough is fine)
2) Recommended gating: per-PR vs nightly vs release tags
3) Sandbox account policy: how many accounts, who owns them, and what guardrails (SCP / budgets / TTL enforcement)

I also want something we can point Security/Compliance to: “these tests are in a dedicated sandbox, logs retained X days, and no prod data.”

Attaching a quick cost model draft from earlier notes.

- Irene
Infra Engineering Lead | Redwood Inference

Attachment: integration-test-cost-estimate.xlsx


---

From: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Initial numbers from the current harness branch (Terraform apply + basic asserts + destroy):

- Wall time: 28–42 min depending on AWS API slowness (NAT GW + endpoints are the long pole)
- Cost: 
  - NAT Gateway: ~$0.045/hr + data processing (low in tests)
  - VPC endpoints: a few $/day equivalent, but per-run it’s non-trivial
  - CloudWatch logs: small
  - Net: I’m seeing ~$12–$25/run (wide band; depends on how long NAT+endpoints are up)

Recommendation:
- Per-PR: keep fmt/validate/tflint/checkov + unit-ish tests (plan-based) only
- Nightly: run full apply/destroy integration suite on main
- Release: run full integration suite on the release tag branch before publishing module versions

Sandbox policy:
- 1 dedicated AWS account for terraform ITs (separate from the dogfood sandbox)
- GitHub Actions OIDC role scoped to only the terraform test workspace + specific regions
- Enforce tags and TTL (we already have “redwood:owner=ci” and “redwood:expires-at=…” in the harness)

Happy to write up in a short policy doc if that helps.

- Sahana


---

From: Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 10:31 AM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

+1 to Sahana’s gating.

On guardrails:
- AWS Budgets: hard alert at $250/mo, soft alert at $150/mo for the test account
- SCP:
  - Deny non-us-west-2 + us-east-1 (pick one region if possible)
  - Deny “CreateInternetGateway” except for the CI role (or force egress patterns)
  - Deny expensive instance families (no p* / g* / etc.) just in case
- CloudTrail on in the account (default) + retain 90 days (S3, Glacier optional). This makes the “no prod creds/data” story easier.
- Config rules are probably overkill; we can do tag enforcement + scheduled janitor lambda.

If we do nightly-only, $250/mo is plenty even with some flakes/retries.

- Devon
Release Eng


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 11:09 AM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Adding release/process angle:

If we make the full integration suite a release prerequisite, we should codify it in the release checklist for the modules (ENG-4883). Something like:
- “Nightly green within last 24h OR manual run green on release commit”

Also we should keep the CI default short for contributor velocity. 30–40 mins per PR will create a ton of churn (plus flake risk from AWS API timeouts).

For alpha: I’d prefer “nightly required” + “on-demand run” via workflow_dispatch so we can ask a pilot team to validate a change before we cut a tag.

- Connor


---

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 12:22 PM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

From Product side, nightly + pre-release is fine as long as we have confidence we’ll catch breaking networking changes before pilots do.

Two asks:
1) Can we include a small set of “installer prereq” checks as part of integration (e.g., endpoints + private DNS behavior) so it aligns with redwood-private-installer preflight work? Even if it’s only validating outputs.
2) Please make the policy explicit that the sandbox account is ONLY for this harness (no ad hoc testing) — we’ve had issues in the past where sandbox gets used for random debugging and breaks cost assumptions.

Thanks,
Vivek


---

From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jun 19, 2025 at 9:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Thanks all.

Decision / plan (unless strong objections by EOD):

A) Gating
- Per-PR (required): terraform fmt/validate + tflint + checkov (ENG-4837) + “plan-based” example validation (no apply)
- Nightly on main (required): full integration apply/assert/destroy in a dedicated sandbox account
- Release (required): either nightly green within 24h OR manual integration run on the release commit (workflow_dispatch)

B) Sandbox accounts
- Create 1 dedicated AWS account: “rdw-terraform-itest”
- Ownership: Infra (me as account owner), CI wiring via Sahana/Devon
- No ad hoc use. If someone needs a sandbox for debugging, use the existing dogfood sandbox.

C) Guardrails
- AWS Budgets: alerts at $150/$250 per month
- SCP: restrict regions + deny expensive compute families + enforce required tags
- TTL janitor: scheduled cleanup for anything past expires-at tag
- CloudTrail enabled (default) + 90 day retention

Action items:
- Irene: file/attach the sandbox account request (INT-1734) + add budget + SCP notes
- Sahana: update CI workflow to move apply suite to nightly + workflow_dispatch
- Devon: draft the short “sandbox policy + guardrails” doc (1 pager) and link from the repo README
- Connor: update release checklist to include the integration gate for alpha

Vivek: re (installer prereqs), yes — we’ll add assertions around required endpoints + private DNS flags to catch obvious misconfigs. Not adding EKS/cluster install in alpha.

- Irene


---

From: Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jun 20, 2025 at 4:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Drafted the 1-pager. Key points included:
- Account purpose + prohibited uses
- Access model (GitHub OIDC role only; no human admin except breakglass)
- Budgets + alerts
- TTL tagging requirements (expires-at must be <= 8 hours by policy)
- Janitor behavior + escalation if cleanup fails

text:
From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Team,

We need to lock a plan for the integration test harness that creates/destroys an ephemeral VPC (ENG-4860) and how we fund + isolate it. Right now the proposal in the PR is “run on every PR,” which I don’t think we can sustain.

Can you reply with:
1) Estimated runtime + $/run (rough is fine)
2) Recommended gating: per-PR vs nightly vs release tags
3) Sandbox account policy: how many accounts, who owns them, and what guardrails (SCP / budgets / TTL enforcement)

I also want something we can point Security/Compliance to: “these tests are in a dedicated sandbox, logs retained X days, and no prod data.”

Attaching a quick cost model draft from earlier notes.

- Irene
Infra Engineering Lead | Redwood Inference

Attachment: integration-test-cost-estimate.xlsx


---

From: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Initial numbers from the current harness branch (Terraform apply + basic asserts + destroy):

- Wall time: 28–42 min depending on AWS API slowness (NAT GW + endpoints are the long pole)
- Cost: 
  - NAT Gateway: ~$0.045/hr + data processing (low in tests)
  - VPC endpoints: a few $/day equivalent, but per-run it’s non-trivial
  - CloudWatch logs: small
  - Net: I’m seeing ~$12–$25/run (wide band; depends on how long NAT+endpoints are up)

Recommendation:
- Per-PR: keep fmt/validate/tflint/checkov + unit-ish tests (plan-based) only
- Nightly: run full apply/destroy integration suite on main
- Release: run full integration suite on the release tag branch before publishing module versions

Sandbox policy:
- 1 dedicated AWS account for terraform ITs (separate from the dogfood sandbox)
- GitHub Actions OIDC role scoped to only the terraform test workspace + specific regions
- Enforce tags and TTL (we already have “redwood:owner=ci” and “redwood:expires-at=…” in the harness)

Happy to write up in a short policy doc if that helps.

- Sahana


---

From: Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 10:31 AM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

+1 to Sahana’s gating.

On guardrails:
- AWS Budgets: hard alert at $250/mo, soft alert at $150/mo for the test account
- SCP:
  - Deny non-us-west-2 + us-east-1 (pick one region if possible)
  - Deny “CreateInternetGateway” except for the CI role (or force egress patterns)
  - Deny expensive instance families (no p* / g* / etc.) just in case
- CloudTrail on in the account (default) + retain 90 days (S3, Glacier optional). This makes the “no prod creds/data” story easier.
- Config rules are probably overkill; we can do tag enforcement + scheduled janitor lambda.

If we do nightly-only, $250/mo is plenty even with some flakes/retries.

- Devon
Release Eng


---

From: Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 11:09 AM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Adding release/process angle:

If we make the full integration suite a release prerequisite, we should codify it in the release checklist for the modules (ENG-4883). Something like:
- “Nightly green within last 24h OR manual run green on release commit”

Also we should keep the CI default short for contributor velocity. 30–40 mins per PR will create a ton of churn (plus flake risk from AWS API timeouts).

For alpha: I’d prefer “nightly required” + “on-demand run” via workflow_dispatch so we can ask a pilot team to validate a change before we cut a tag.

- Connor


---

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 12:22 PM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

From Product side, nightly + pre-release is fine as long as we have confidence we’ll catch breaking networking changes before pilots do.

Two asks:
1) Can we include a small set of “installer prereq” checks as part of integration (e.g., endpoints + private DNS behavior) so it aligns with redwood-private-installer preflight work? Even if it’s only validating outputs.
2) Please make the policy explicit that the sandbox account is ONLY for this harness (no ad hoc testing) — we’ve had issues in the past where sandbox gets used for random debugging and breaks cost assumptions.

Thanks,
Vivek


---

From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Sahana Iyer <sahana_iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Talia Benson <talia_benson@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jun 19, 2025 at 9:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Thanks all.

Decision / plan (unless strong objections by EOD):

A) Gating
- Per-PR (required): terraform fmt/validate + tflint + checkov (ENG-4837) + “plan-based” example validation (no apply)
- Nightly on main (required): full integration apply/assert/destroy in a dedicated sandbox account
- Release (required): either nightly green within 24h OR manual integration run on the release commit (workflow_dispatch)

B) Sandbox accounts
- Create 1 dedicated AWS account: “rdw-terraform-itest”
- Ownership: Infra (me as account owner), CI wiring via Sahana/Devon
- No ad hoc use. If someone needs a sandbox for debugging, use the existing dogfood sandbox.

C) Guardrails
- AWS Budgets: alerts at $150/$250 per month
- SCP: restrict regions + deny expensive compute families + enforce required tags
- TTL janitor: scheduled cleanup for anything past expires-at tag
- CloudTrail enabled (default) + 90 day retention

Action items:
- Irene: file/attach the sandbox account request (INT-1734) + add budget + SCP notes
- Sahana: update CI workflow to move apply suite to nightly + workflow_dispatch
- Devon: draft the short “sandbox policy + guardrails” doc (1 pager) and link from the repo README
- Connor: update release checklist to include the integration gate for alpha

Vivek: re (installer prereqs), yes — we’ll add assertions around required endpoints + private DNS flags to catch obvious misconfigs. Not adding EKS/cluster install in alpha.

- Irene


---

From: Devon Price <devon_price@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Fri, Jun 20, 2025 at 4:05 PM PT
Subject: Re: CI budget + sandbox accounts for Terraform integration tests (ephemeral VPC)

Drafted the 1-pager. Key points included:
- Account purpose + prohibited uses
- Access model (GitHub OIDC role only; no human admin except breakglass)
- Budgets + alerts
- TTL tagging requirements (expires-at must be <= 8 hours by policy)
- Janitor behavior + escalation if cleanup fails

Attaching for review. Once approved I’ll add to Confluence and link in the repo docs.

title: Escalation: Rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage for Redwood Private (Cascade Financial Group)
preview:
From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa.chen@cascadefg.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Becker <tom.becker@cascadefg.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Escalation: rollback guarantees + upgrade support coverage (Redwood Private)

Markus,

We’re preparing our next Redwood Private upgrade in our VPC environment and need written clarity on two items before we’ll schedule the maintenance window:

1) Rollback guarantees: if the upgrade fails (including DB migration issues), can we roll back to the prior version and what is the expected time to restore service? We need something more concrete than “best effort.”

2) Support coverage: we will be doing this in our standard maintenance window (Saturday 1:00–4:00am ET). We need to know if Redwood will have engineering/SRE coverage during the window, and what escalation path exists if rollback is required.

We’ve had issues with other vendors where “rollback” meant “rebuild the cluster,” which is not acceptable for us.

Please respond by EOD Thursday so we can confirm the window with our change advisory board.

Thanks,
Alyssa

—
Alyssa Chen
Director, Platform Operations
Cascade Financial Group

For support coverage:

- If they want this as a contractual commitment, it needs to be captured as a paid premium support addendum / named escalation path, but for this upcoming window we can do it as a one-off (assuming 48h notice and precheck outputs shared beforehand).

I’d avoid using the word “guarantee” on RTO/RPO. We can give typical timings and what determines rollback time (snapshot restore vs logical restore).

Sean
